[1] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.1)
SANSKRIT: वृद्धिशब्दः संज्ञात्वेन विधीयते प्रत्येकमादैचां वर्णानां सामान्येन तद्भावितानामतद्भावितानां च ।
ENGLISH: आ, ऐ and औ are called _vriddhi_.

[2] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.2)
SANSKRIT: गुणशब्दः संज्ञात्वेन विधीयते प्रत्येकमदेङां वर्णानां सामान्येन तद्भावितानामतद्भावितानाञ्च ॥
ENGLISH: अ, ए and ओ are called _guna_

[3] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.3)
SANSKRIT: वृद्धिगुणौ स्वसंज्ञया (वृद्ध्या गुणेन वा) शिष्यमाणाविक एव स्थाने वेदितव्यौ ॥
ENGLISH: In the absence of any special rule, whenever _guna_ or _vriddhi_ is enjoined about any expression by using the terms _guna_ or _vriddhi_, it is to be understood to come in the room of the _ik_ vowels only. (_i_, _u_, _ri_, and _li_ long and short,) of that expression.

[4] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.4)
SANSKRIT: धात्वेकदेशो धातुस्तस्य लोपो यस्मिन्नार्द्धधातुके तदार्द्धधातुकं धातुलोपं तत्र ये गुणवृद्धी प्राप्नुतस्ते न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The _Guna_ and _vriddhi_ substitutions, which otherwise would have presented themselves, do not take place, when such an _ardhadhatuka_ (III.4.114) affix follows, which causes a portion of the root to be elided.

[5] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.5)
SANSKRIT: क्ङिन्निमित्ते ये गुणवृद्धी प्राप्नुतस्ते न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: And that, which otherwise would have caused _guna_ or _vriddhi_, does not do so, when it has an indicatory क् ग् or ङ्.

[6] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.6)
SANSKRIT: दीधीवेव्योरिटश्चये गुणवृद्धी प्राप्नुतस्ते न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The _guna_ and _vriddhi_ substitutions, which would otherwise have presented themselves, do not however come, in the place of the vowels of _didhi_ 'to shine,' and _vevi_ 'to go,' and of the augment called इट्.

[7] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.7)
SANSKRIT: भिन्नजातीयैरज्भिरव्यवहिताः श्लिष्टोच्चारिता हलः संयोगसंज्ञा भवन्ति समुदायः संज्ञी ॥
ENGLISH: Consonants unseparated by a vowel are called conjunct consonants.

[8] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.8)
SANSKRIT: मुखसहिता नासिका मुखनासिका, तया य उच्चार्यते वर्णः सोऽनुनासिकसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which is pronounced by the nose along with the mouth is called _Anunåsika_ or nasal.

[9] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.9)
SANSKRIT: तुल्य आस्ये प्रयत्नो यस्य वर्णस्य येन वर्णेन सह स समानजातीयं प्रति सवर्णसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Those whose place of utterance and effort are equal are called _savarna_ or homogeneous letters.

[10] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.10)
SANSKRIT: तुल्यास्यप्रयत्नावपि अज्झलौ परस्परं सवर्णसंज्ञौ न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: There is however no homogeneity between vowels and consonants, though their place and effort be equal.

[11] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.11)
SANSKRIT: ईत् उत् एत् इत्येवमन्तं द्विवचनं शब्दरूपं प्रगृह्यसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A dual case affix ending in ई or ऊ or ए is called _Pragrihya_, or excepted vowels which do not admit of _sandhi_ or conjunction.

[12] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.12)
SANSKRIT: अदसः सम्बन्धी यो मकारस्तस्मात् पर ईदूदेतः प्रगृह्यसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: (The same letters) after the म् of the pronoun अदस् are _Pragrihya_.

[13] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.13)
SANSKRIT: शे इत्येतत् प्रगृह्यसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affix शे, (the Vedic substitute of the case-affixes), is a _pragrihya_.

[14] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.14)
SANSKRIT: एकश्चासावच्च एकाच् निपातो य एकाच् आङ्वर्जितः स प्रगृह्यसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A particle (I.4.56) consisting of a single vowel, with the exception of the particle आङ् is a _Pragrihya_.

[15] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.15)
SANSKRIT: ओदन्तो यो निपातः स प्रगृह्यसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The final ओ of a particle is a _pragrihya_.

[16] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.16)
SANSKRIT: सम्बुद्धिनिमित्तो य ओकारः स शाकल्यस्याचार्यस्य मतेन प्रगृह्यसंज्ञो भवति, इति शब्देऽनार्षे अवैदिके परतः ॥
ENGLISH: The final ओ of the vocative singular (II.3.49) before the word इति according to _Sakalya_, in secular or non-vedic literature, is _pragrihya_.

[17] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.17)
SANSKRIT: उञः प्रगृह्यसंज्ञा भवति इतौ शाकल्यस्याचार्यस्य मतेन ॥
ENGLISH: The particle उञ् before _iti_, according to _Sakalya_ is a _Pragrihya_.

[18] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.18)
SANSKRIT: उञ इतावनार्षे ऊँ इत्ययमादेशो भवति दीर्घोऽनुनासिकः शाकल्यस्य मतेन प्रगृह्यसंज्ञकश्च ॥
ENGLISH: The particle ऊँ replaces उञ् in non-vedic literature, and it is a _pragrihya_ in the opinion of _Sakalya_.

[19] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.19)
SANSKRIT: ईदन्तमूदन्तञ्च शब्दरूपं सप्तम्यर्थे वर्त्तमानं प्रगृह्यसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The final ई and ऊ of words giving the sense of the locative case are _pragrihya_.

[20] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.20)
SANSKRIT: दारूपाश्चत्वारो धातवो धारूपौ च द्वौ दाब्दैपौ वर्जयित्वा घुसंज्ञका भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The verbs having the form of _dà_ 'to give' and _dhā_ 'to place,' are called _ghu_.

[21] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.21)
SANSKRIT: आदाविव अन्तइव एकस्मिन्नपि कार्यं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An operation should be performed on a single letter, as upon an initial or upon a final.

[22] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.22)
SANSKRIT: तरप् तमप् इत्येतौ प्रत्ययौ घ संज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes _tarap_ and _tamap_ are called घ.

[23] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.23)
SANSKRIT: बहुगणवतुडति इत्येते संख्यासंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _bahu_ 'many,' _gana_ 'class,' and the words ending in the affix _vatu_ (V. 2. 39), and _dati_ (V. 2. 41) are called numerals (_sankhya_).

[24] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.24)
SANSKRIT: षकारान्ता नकारान्ता च या संख्या सा षट्संज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _Sankhyas_ having ष or न as their final are called _shat_.

[25] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.25)
SANSKRIT: डत्यन्ता या संख्या सा षट्संज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the _sankhyas_ ending with the affix _dati_ are called _shat_.

[26] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.26)
SANSKRIT: क्तश्च क्तवतुश्च क्तक्तवतू प्रत्ययौ निष्ठासंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes _kta_ and _ktavatu_ are called _Nishtha_.

[27] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.27)
SANSKRIT: सर्वशब्द आदिर्येषां तानीमानि सर्वादीनि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _sarva_, 'all,' and the rest are called _sarvanama_ or pronouns.

[28] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.28)
SANSKRIT: दिशां समासो दिक्समासो दिगुपदिष्टे समासे बहुव्रीहौ विभाषा सर्वादीनि सर्वनाम संज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The above words are optionally _sarvanama_ when they occur in a _bahuvrihi_ compound signifying direction (II. 2. 26.)

[29] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.29)
SANSKRIT: बहुव्रीहौ समासे सर्वादीनि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि न भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _sarva_ &c., are not _sarvanama_ when occurring in a _bahuvrihi_ compound.

[30] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.30)
SANSKRIT: तृतीयासमासे सर्वादीनि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि न भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: In Instrumental Determinative Compounds the words _sarva_ &c. are not _sarvanama_.

[31] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.31)
SANSKRIT: द्वन्द्वे च समासे सर्वादीनि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि न भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: And in Collective Compound, (II. 2. 26) the words _sarva_ &c., are not _sarvanama_.

[32] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.32)
SANSKRIT: द्वन्द्वे समासे जसि विभाषा सर्वादीनि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि न भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: (Their _dvandva_ compounds) are optionally _sarvanama_ when the nominative plural termination _jas_ follows.

[33] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.33)
SANSKRIT: प्रथम चरम तय अल्प अर्द्ध कतिपय नेम इत्येतेजसि विभाषा सर्वनामसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: And also the words _prathama_ 'first'; _charama_ 'last,' words ending with the affix _taya_ (V. 2. 42); _alpa_ 'few' _ardha_ 'half' _katipaya_ 'some,' and _nema_ 'half' are optionally _sarvanama_, before the nom. pl. termination.

[34] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.34)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वादीनि विभाषा जसि सर्वनामसंज्ञानि भवन्ति व्यवस्थायामसंज्ञायाम् ॥
ENGLISH: The words _purva_ 'prior', _para_ 'after,' avara 'posterior,' _dakshina_ 'south,' _uttara_ 'north.' _apara_ 'other,' and _adhara_ 'inferior,' when they discriminate relative position, not when they are appellatives, are optionally _sarvanama_ before the affix _jas_.

[35] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.35)
SANSKRIT: स्वमित्येतच्छब्दरूपं जसि विभाषा सर्वनामसंज्ञं भवति न चेज् ज्ञाति धनयोः संज्ञारूपेण वर्त्तते ॥
ENGLISH: The word _sva_ 'own,' when it does not mean a kinsman or property is optionally _sarvanama_ before the affix _jas_.

[36] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.36)
SANSKRIT: अन्तरमित्येतच्छब्दरूपं विभाषा जसि सर्वनामसंज्ञं भवति बहिर्योग उपसंव्याने गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: The word _antara_ being always a _sarvanama_, when meaning 'outer' or 'a lower garment,' is optionally so before the affix _jas_.

[37] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.37)
SANSKRIT: स्वरादीनि शब्दरूपाणि निपाताश्चाव्ययसंज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _svar_ 'heaven,' &c., and the particles (I. 4. 56) are called indeclinables.

[38] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.38)
SANSKRIT: तद्धितान्तः शब्दोऽसर्वविभक्तिरव्ययसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the words ending in _taddhita_ or secondary affixes (IV. 1. 76.) which are not declined in all the cases are also indeclinables.

[39] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.39)
SANSKRIT: कृद्यो मकारान्त एजन्तश्च तदन्तं शब्दरूपमव्ययसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words formed by those _krit_ or primary affixes, (III. 1. 93) which end with म् or in ए, ओ, ऐ and औ are also indeclinables.

[40] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.40)
SANSKRIT: क्त्वा तोसुन् कसुन् इत्येवमन्तं शब्दरूपमव्ययसंज्ञम्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words ending with _ktva_, _tosun_ (III. 4. 16) and _kasun_ are indeclinables.

[41] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.41)
SANSKRIT: अव्ययीभावसमासोऽव्ययसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: (The compound called) _Avyayibhava_ (II. 1. 5) is also indeclinable.

[42] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.42)
SANSKRIT: शि इत्येतत्सर्वनामस्थानसंज्ञम्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affix शि (VII. I. 20) is called _sarvanamasthana_.

[43] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.43)
SANSKRIT: सुडिति पञ्च वचनानि सर्वनामस्थानसंज्ञानि भवंति नपुंसकादन्यत्र ॥
ENGLISH: The first five case affixes comprised in the _pratyahara_ _sut_. (i. e., the three case-terminations of the nominative, and the singular and dual of the accusative) are called also _sarvanamasthana_; except the case-affixes of the neuter gender.

[44] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.44)
SANSKRIT: नेति प्रतिषेधो वेति विकल्पस्तयोः प्रतिषेधविकल्पयोर्विभाषेति संज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: 'May or may not' is called _vibhasha_ or option.

[45] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.45)
SANSKRIT: इग्यो यणः स्थाने भूतो भावी वा तस्य संप्रसारणमित्येषा संज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _ik_ vowels which replace the semi-vowels _yan_ are called _samprasarana_.

[46] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.46)
SANSKRIT: आदिष्टिद्भवति अन्तः किद्भवति षष्ठी निर्दिष्टस्य ॥
ENGLISH: Of whatsoever the augments enunciated are distinguished by an indicatory ट् or क्, they precede or follow it accordingly.

[47] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.47)
SANSKRIT: अचां सन्निविष्टानामन्त्यादचः परो मिद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The augment that has an indicatory म् comes after the last among the vowels, and becomes the final position of that which it augments.

[48] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.48)
SANSKRIT: एचो हृस्वादेशे कर्तव्यइगेव हृस्वो भवति नान्यः ॥
ENGLISH: Of एच् vowels, इक् is the substitute, when short is to be substituted.

[49] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.49)
SANSKRIT: इह शास्त्रे या षष्ठी अनियतयोगा श्रूयते सा स्थानेयोगैव भवति नान्ययोगा स्थानेयोगनिमित्तभूते सति सा प्रतिपत्तव्या ॥
ENGLISH: The force of the genitive case in a _sutra_ is that of the phrase 'in the place of' when no special rules qualify the sense of the genitive.

[50] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.50)
SANSKRIT: स्थाने प्राप्यमाणानामन्तरतम आदेशो भवति सदृशतमः ॥
ENGLISH: When a common term is obtained as a substitute, the likest of its significates to that in the place of which it comes, is the actual substitute.

[51] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.51)
SANSKRIT: उः (ॠकारस्य) स्थानेऽण् प्रसज्यमान एव रपरो वेदितव्यः ॥
ENGLISH: When a letter of अण् _pratyahara_ comes as a substitute for ॠ it is always followed by a र.

[52] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.52)
SANSKRIT: षष्ठीनिर्दिष्टस्य य उच्यते आदेशः सोन्त्यस्यालः स्थाने वेदितव्यः ॥
ENGLISH: The substitute takes the place of only the final letter (of that which is denoted by a term exhibited in the genitive or sixth case).

[53] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.53)
SANSKRIT: ङिच्च य आदेशः सोऽनेकालपि अलोन्त्यस्य भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the substitute which has an indicatory ङ (even though it consists of more than one letter) takes the place of the final letter only of the original expression.

[54] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.54)
SANSKRIT: परस्य कार्यं शिष्यमाणमादेरलः प्रत्येतव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: That which is enjoined to come in the room of what follows is to be understood as coming in the room only of the first letter thereof.

[55] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.55)
SANSKRIT: अनेकाल् य आदेशः शिच्च सर्वस्य षष्ठी निर्दिष्टस्य स्थाने भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A substitute consisting of more than one letter, and a substitute having an indicatory श take the place of the whole of the original expression exhibited in the sixth case.

[56] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.56)
SANSKRIT: स्थानिवदादेशो भवति स्थान्याश्रयेषु कार्येष्वनलाश्रयेषु स्थान्यलाश्रयाणि कार्याणि वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: A substitute (_adesa_) is like the former occupant (_sthani_) but not in the case of a rule the occasion for the operation of which is furnished by the letters of the original term.

[57] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.57)
SANSKRIT: अजादेशः परनिमित्तकः पूर्वविधौ कर्त्तव्ये स्थानिवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A substitute in the room of a vowel caused by something that follows, should be regarded as that whose place it takes when a rule would else take effect on what stands anterior to the original vowel.

[58] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.58)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वेणातिप्रसक्तः स्थानिवद्भाव एतेषु विधिषु प्रतिषिध्यते ॥
ENGLISH: Not so, in rules relating to the finals of words, to the doubling of letters, to the affixing of _varach_, to the elision of _ya_, to accent, to homogeneous letters, to _anusvara_, to the lengthening of vowels, and to the substitution of _jas_ and _char_ letters.

[59] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.59)
SANSKRIT: द्विर्वचननिमित्तेऽचि अजादेशः स्थानिवद्भवति द्विर्वचन एव कर्त्तव्ये ॥
ENGLISH: Before an affix having an initial vowel, which causes reduplication, the substitute which takes the place of a vowel is like the original vowel even in form, only for the purposes of reduplication and no further.

[60] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.60)
SANSKRIT: अदर्शनमश्रवणमनुच्चारणमनुपलब्धिरभावो वर्णविनाश इत्यनर्थान्तरमेतैः शब्दैर्योर्थोऽभिधीयते तस्य लोप इतीयं संज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The substitution of a blank (_lopa_) signifies disappearance.

[61] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.61)
SANSKRIT: प्रत्ययादर्शनस्य लुक् श्लु लुप् इत्येताः संज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The disappearance of an affix when it is caused by the words _luk_, _slu_ or _lup_ are designated by those terms respectively.

[62] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.62)
SANSKRIT: प्रत्ययलोपे कृते प्रत्ययलक्षणं प्रत्ययहेतुकं कार्यं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When elision of an affix has taken place (_lopa_), the affix still exerts its influence, and the operations dependant upon it, take place as if it were present.

[63] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.63)
SANSKRIT: लुमता शब्देन लुप्ते प्रत्यये यदङ्गं तस्य प्रत्ययलक्षणं कार्यं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the base (_anga_), whose affix has been elided by the use of any of the three words containing लु, the operations dependent on it do not take place, regarding such base.

[64] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.64)
SANSKRIT: अचां संनिविष्टानां योऽन्त्योच् तदादि शब्दरूपं टिसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The final portion of a word, beginning with the last among the vowels in the word, is called टि.

[65] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.65)
SANSKRIT: धात्वादौवर्ण समुदायेऽन्त्यादलः पूर्वोयो वर्णः सोऽलेवोपधा संज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The letter immediately preceding the last letter of a word is called the penultimate.

[66] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.66)
SANSKRIT: तस्मिन्निति सप्तम्यर्थनिर्देशे पूर्वस्यैव कार्यं भवति नोत्तरस्य ॥
ENGLISH: When a term is exhibited in the seventh case in these _sutras_, the operation directed, is to be understood as affecting the state of what immediately precedes that which the term denotes.

[67] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.67)
SANSKRIT: तस्मादिति पञ्चम्यर्थनिर्देश उत्तरस्यैव कार्यं भवति न पूर्वस्य ॥
ENGLISH: An operation caused by the exhibition of a term in the ablative or fifth case, is to be understood to enjoin the substitution of something in the room of that which immediately follows the word denoted by the term.

[68] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.68)
SANSKRIT: शास्त्रे स्वमेव रूपं शब्दस्य ग्राह्यं वोध्यंप्रत्याय्यं भवति न वाह्योऽर्थः शब्दसंज्ञां वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: In this Grammar, when an operation is directed with regard to a word, the individual form of the word possessing meaning is to be understood, except with regard to a word which is a definition.

[69] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.69)
SANSKRIT: अण् गृह्यमाण उदिच्च सवर्णानां ग्राहको भवति स्वस्य च रूपस्य प्रत्ययं वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: The letters of the _pratyahara_ अण् i. e. the vowels and semi-vowels, and a term having उ for its indicatory letter, refer to their own form as well as to their homogeneous letters, except when they are used as _pratyayas_ or affixes.

[70] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.70)
SANSKRIT: तपरो वर्णस्तत्कालस्यात्मनातुल्यकालस्य गुणान्तरयुक्तस्य सवर्णस्य ग्राहको भवति स्वस्य च रूपस्य ॥
ENGLISH: The letter which has त् after or before it, besides referring to its own form, refers to those homogeneous letters which have the same prosodial length or time.

[71] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.71)
SANSKRIT: आदिरन्त्येनेत्संज्ञकेन सह गृह्यमाणस्तन्मध्यपतितानां वर्णानां ग्राहको भवति स्वस्य च रूपस्य ॥
ENGLISH: An initial letter, with a final इत् letter as a final, is the name of itself and of the intervening letters.

[72] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.72)
SANSKRIT: येन विशेषणेन विधिर्विधीयते सतदन्तस्यात्मान्तस्य समुदायस्य ग्राहको भवति स्वस्य च रूपस्य ॥
ENGLISH: An injunction which is made with regard to a particular attribute, applies to words having that attribute at their end as well as to that attribute itself.

[73] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.73)
SANSKRIT: अर्चामध्ये यस्य वृद्धिसंज्ञक आदिभूतस्तच्छब्दरूपं वृद्धसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That word, among the vowels of which the first is a _vriddhi_, is called _vriddham_.

[74] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.74)
SANSKRIT: त्यदादीनि शब्दरूपाणि वृद्धसंज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _tyad_ &c., are also called _vriddham_.

[75] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.1.75)
SANSKRIT: एङ् यस्याचामादिस्तत्प्राग्देशाभिधाने वृद्ध संज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word that has the letters ए and ओ as the first among its vowels, gets also the designation of _vriddham_, when it is the name of Eastern countries.

[76] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.1)
SANSKRIT: गाङ्कुटादिभ्यः परेऽञ्णितः प्रत्यया ङितो भवन्ति, ङिद्वद्भवन्तीत्यर्थः ॥
ENGLISH: All affixes after the verb _gan_ 'to study' and the verb _kut_ 'to be crooked,' and the rest, are as if they had an indicatory ङ् (_nit_), except those affixes which have an indicatory ञ् (_nit_) or ण् (_nit_).

[77] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.2)
SANSKRIT: ओविजी भयचलनयोः अस्मात्पर इडादिः प्रत्ययो ङिद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An affix that begins with the augment इट् (VII. 2. 35) is _nit_ after the root _vij_., 'to fear, to move.'

[78] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.3)
SANSKRIT: ऊर्णुच् आच्छादने अस्मात्पर इडादिः प्रत्ययो विभाषा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _urna_ 'to cover,' the affix beginning with the augment इट् is regarded optionally like _nit_.

[79] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.4)
SANSKRIT: सार्वधातुकं यदपित्तन्ङिद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Sarvadhatuka_ (III. 4. 113) affix not having an indicatory प् is like _nit_.

[80] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.5)
SANSKRIT: असंयोगान्ताद्धातोः परो लिट् प्रत्ययोऽपित् किट्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes of _Lit_ (Perfect Tense) not coming after a conjunct consonant, are as if they had an indicatory क् (_kit_).

[81] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.6)
SANSKRIT: इन्धिभवतीत्येताभ्यां परो लिट् प्रत्ययः किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _Lit_ or Perfect Tense affixes after the roots _indhi_ 'to kindle,' and _bhu_ 'to become,' also are _kit_.

[82] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.7)
SANSKRIT: मृड मृद गुध कुष क्लिश वद वस् इत्येतेभ्यः परः क्त्वाप्रत्ययः किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affix _ktva_ is _kit_ after _mrid_ 'to be gracious,' _mrid_ ' to squeeze,' _gudh_ 'to wrap up,' _kush_ 'to tear,' _klis_ 'to suffer,' _vad_ 'to speak' and _vas_ 'to dwell.'

[83] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.8)
SANSKRIT: रुद विद मुष ग्रहि स्वपि प्रच्छ इत्येतेभ्यः संश्च क्त्वा च कितौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes _ktva_ and _san_ (III. 1. 7) are _kit_, after _rud_ 'to weep,' _vid_ 'to know,' _mush_ 'to steal,' _grah_ 'to seize,' _svap_ 'to sleep,' and _prachchh_ 'to ask.'

[84] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.9)
SANSKRIT: इगन्ताद्धातोः परो झलादिः सन् किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affix _san_ beginning with a letter of the _Pratyahara_ _jhal_ is like _kit_, after verbs ending in _ik_ vowels.

[85] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.10)
SANSKRIT: इगन्तादिक्समीपाद्धलः परः सन् झलादिः किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And after a root ending in a consonant, and preceded by a vowel of the _pratyahara_ _ik_, the affix _san_ beginning with a _jhal_ consonant, is like _kit_.

[86] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.11)
SANSKRIT: इगन्तादिक्समीपाद्धलः परौ झलादी लिङ्सिचौ आत्मनेपदेषु परतः कितौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: After roots ending in a consonant, that adjoins a vowel of _ik_ _pratyahara_, the substitutes of _lin_ (III. 3. 161) and _sich_ (III. 1. 44) when they begin with a consonant of _jhal_ _pratyahara_, are _kit_, when the _atmanepada_ affixes follow.

[87] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.12)
SANSKRIT: ऋवर्णान्ताद्धातोः परौ लिङ्सिचावात्मनेपदेषु झलादी कितौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: And after verbs ending in ॠ, the substitutes of _lin_ and the affix _sich_, are _kit_, when they begin with _jhal_, and the _Atmanepada_ affixes follow.

[88] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.13)
SANSKRIT: गमेर्धातोः परौ लिङ्सिचावात्मनेपदेषु झलादी वा कितौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: Optionally after the verb _gam_, the _lin_ and _sich_ beginning with _jhal_ consonants, in the _Atmanepada_, are _kit_.

[89] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.14)
SANSKRIT: हन्तेर्धातोः परः सिच् किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _sich_ _Atmanepada_ affixes are _kit_ after the root _han_ 'to kill.'

[90] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.15)
SANSKRIT: यमेर्धातोर्गन्धने वर्त्तमानात्परः सिच् प्रत्ययः कित् भवति आत्मनेपदेषु परतः ॥
ENGLISH: The affix _sich_ before _Atmanepada_ affixes, is _kit_ after the verb _yam_, when meaning 'to divulge.'

[91] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.16)
SANSKRIT: यमेर्धातोरुपयमने वर्त्तमानात् परः सिच् प्रत्ययो विभाषा किद्भवति आत्मनेपदेषु परतः ॥
ENGLISH: The _sich_ before _atmanepada_ affixes is _kit_ optionally, when _yam_ means 'to espouse.'

[92] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.17)
SANSKRIT: तिष्ठतेर्धातोः धुसंज्ञकानाञ्च इकारश्चान्तादेशः सिच्च किद्भवति आत्मनेपदेषु परतः ॥
ENGLISH: The affix _sich_ before _Atmanepada_ affixes, is _kit_ after _stha_'to stand' and _ghu_ (I. 1. 20) verbs, and these verbs change their आ into इ before these terminations.

[93] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.18)
SANSKRIT: क्त्वाप्रत्ययः सेण्न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affix _ktva_ when it takes the augment इट् (i.e. when it is _set_) is not _kit_.

[94] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.19)
SANSKRIT: शीङ् स्विदि मिदि क्ष्विदि धृष् इत्येतेभ्यः परो निष्ठा प्रत्ययः सेण् न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _Nishtha_ affixes are not _kit_ after the verbs _si_ 'to lie down,' _svid_ 'to sweat,' _mid_ 'to melt,' _kshvid_ 'to be unctuous,' and _dhrish_ 'to offend.'

[95] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.20)
SANSKRIT: मृषेर्धातोः तितिक्षायामर्थे निष्ठासेण् न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _Nishtha_ is not _kit_ after the verb _mrish_ when meaning 'to forbear.'

[96] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.21)
SANSKRIT: उदुपधाद्धातोः परो भावे आदिकर्मणि च वर्त्तमानो निष्ठाप्रत्ययः सेडन्यतरस्यां न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _Nishtha_ is optionally not _kit_, after the verbs with a penultimate उ if used impersonally or denoting the beginning of action.

[97] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.22)
SANSKRIT: पूङः परो निष्ठा प्रत्ययः क्त्वा च सेण्न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _Nishtha_ and the _set_ _ktva_ are not _kit_ after the verb _pun_ 'to purify.'

[98] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.23)
SANSKRIT: नकारोपधाद्धातोस्थकारान्तात्फकारान्ताच्च परः क्त्वा प्रत्ययः सेड् वा न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _ktva_ is optionally _kit_, after the verbs having a penultimate न् and ending in थ् and फ्.

[99] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.24)
SANSKRIT: वञ्चि लुञ्चि ऋत् इत्येतेभ्यः परः क्त्वा प्रत्ययः सेड्वा न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _ktva_ is optionally _kit_, after the verbs _vanch_ 'to cheat' _lunch_ 'to pluck' and _rit_ 'to dare or abhor.'

[100] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.25)
SANSKRIT: तृषि मृषि कृषि इत्येतेभ्यः परः क्त्वा प्रत्ययः सेट् काश्यपस्याचार्यस्य मते वा न किद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _set_ _ktva_ is optionally _kit_, according to the opinion of _Rishi_ _Kasyapa_, after the verbs _trish_ 'to be thirsty,' _mrish_ 'to sprinkle,' and _kris_ 'to become lean.'

[101] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.26)
SANSKRIT: उकारोपधादिकारोपधाच्चधातो रलन्ताद्धलादेः परः संश्च क्त्वाच सेटौ वा कितौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: After a verb which begins with a consonant, and ends in a letter of _Ral_ _pratyahara_, and has as its penultimate इ or ई, उ or ऊ, the _ktva_ and _san_ affixes are optionally _kit_.

[102] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.27)
SANSKRIT: उ ऊ ऊ३ इत्येवं कालोऽज् हृस्वदीर्घप्लुत इत्येवं संज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A vowel whose time is that of short _u_, long _u_ and the prolated _u_, is called respectively _hrasva_ short, _dirgha_ long, and _pluta_ prolated.

[103] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.28)
SANSKRIT: हृस्व दीर्घ प्लुताः स्वसंज्ञायाशिष्यमाणा अच एव स्थाने वेदितव्याः ॥
ENGLISH: The short, long, and prolated, when enunciated as such, by using these terms, are to be understood to come in the place of vowels only.

[104] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.29)
SANSKRIT: उच्चैरुपलभ्यमानो योअच् स उदात्तसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The vowel that is perceived as having a high tone is called _Udatta_ or acutely accented.

[105] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.30)
SANSKRIT: नीचैरुपलभ्यमानो योऽच् सोनुदात्तसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The vowel that is perceived as having a low tone is called _Anudatta_ or gravely accented.

[106] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.31)
SANSKRIT: उदात्तानुदात्तस्वरसमाहारो योऽच् स स्वरितसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The vowel that has the combination of _Udatta_ and _Anudatta_ tones is said to be _svarita_ or circumflexly accented.

[107] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.32)
SANSKRIT: तस्य स्वरितस्य आदावर्द्धहृस्वमुदात्तम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of it (_svarita_) the first portion is _udatta_, to the extent of a half measure, of prosodial length.

[108] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.33)
SANSKRIT: एकश्रुति वाक्यं भवति दूरात्सम्बुद्धौ सति ॥
ENGLISH: In addressing a person from a distance, the tone is called _Ekasruti_ or monotony.

[109] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.34)
SANSKRIT: जपन्यूङ्खसामानि वर्जयित्वा यज्ञकर्मणि मन्त्राणामैकश्रुत्यं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In sacrificial works, there is Monotony, except in _japa_ (silent repetition of a formula), _Nyunkha_ vowels (sixteen sorts of _om_) and the _Sama_ _vedas_.

[110] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.35)
SANSKRIT: यज्ञकर्मणि वषट्कारः उच्चैस्तरां वा भवति एक श्रुतिर्वा ॥
ENGLISH: The pronunciation of the word _vashat_ may optionally be by raising the voice (acutely accented), or it may be pronounced with monotony.

[111] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.36)
SANSKRIT: छन्दसि विषये विभाषा एक श्रुतिर्भवति पक्षान्तरे त्रैस्वर्यमेव भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The Monotony is optional in the recitation of the Vedas, or they may be recited with accents.

[112] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.37)
SANSKRIT: सुब्रह्मण्यायामेकश्रुतिर्नभवति । यस्तु लक्षणप्राप्तः स्वरितस्तस्योदात्त आदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: There should be no Monotony in the recitation of the _Subrahmanya_ hymns and in those hymns, the vowels, that would otherwise have taken the _svarita_ accent, take the _udatta_ accent instead.

[113] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.38)
SANSKRIT: देवब्रह्मणोः स्वरितस्यानु दात्त आदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _deva_ and _Brahmana_ in those hymns have _anudatta_ accent.

[114] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.39)
SANSKRIT: संहितायाम् विषये स्वरितात्परेषामनुदात्तानामेकश्रुतिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The Monotony takes the place of the _anudatta_ vowels which follow the _svarita_ vowels, in close proximity (_sanhita_).

[115] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.40)
SANSKRIT: उदात्तपरस्य स्वरितपरस्य चानुदात्तस्य सन्नतरआदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The accent called _Sannatara_ is substituted in the room of an _anudatta_ vowel, which has an _udatta_ or _svarita_ vowel following it.

[116] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.41)
SANSKRIT: अपृक्त इतीयं संज्ञा भवति एकाल् यः प्रत्ययस्तस्य ॥
ENGLISH: An affix consisting of a single letter is called an _Aprikta_.

[117] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.42)
SANSKRIT: तत्पुरुषः समानाधिकरणपदः कर्मधारयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound, the case of each member of which is the same, is called _karmadharaya_ or Appositional Determinative compound.

[118] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.43)
SANSKRIT: प्रथमया विभक्तया यन्निर्दिश्यते समासशास्त्रे तदुपसर्जनसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In (this book, in the _sutras_ relating to) compound, the word that is exhibited in the first (Nominative) case, is called _upasarjana_ or the secondary word.

[119] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.44)
SANSKRIT: एका विभक्तिर्यस्य तदिदमेक विभक्ति, समासे विधीयमाने यन्नियतविभक्तिकं द्वितीये सम्बन्धिनि बाहुभिर्विभक्तिभिर्युज्यमानेप्येकयैविवभक्त्या युज्यते तदुपसर्जनसंज्ञं भवति, अपूर्वनिपाते, पूर्वनिपातं पूर्वनिपाताख्यमुपसर्जनकार्यं वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: A word which has one fixed case, (while the word compounded with it may vary its case) is also _upasarjana_, except for the purposes of the rule which requires the _upasarjana_ to stand first in a compound.

[120] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.45)
SANSKRIT: अर्थवच्छब्दरूपं प्रातिपदिकसंज्ञं भवति धातुप्रत्ययौ वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: A significant form of a word, not being a verbal root (_dhatu_), or an affix (_pratyaya_) is called a _pratipadika_ or crude-form.

[121] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.46)
SANSKRIT: कृतस्तद्धिताः समासाश्च प्रातिपदिकसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The forms ending in _Krit_ affixes, or _Taddhita_ affixes, or compound are also called _Pratipadika_.

[122] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.47)
SANSKRIT: नपुंसकलिङ्गेऽर्थे यत्प्रातिपदिकं वर्त्तते तस्य हृस्वो भवति आदेशोलोन्त्यस्याचः ॥
ENGLISH: The short vowel is substituted in the neuter, for the final vowel, of a _pratipadika_.

[123] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.48)
SANSKRIT: उपसर्जनगोशब्दान्तस्य उपसर्जनस्त्रीप्रत्ययान्तस्य च प्रातिपदिकस्य हृस्वो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A short vowel is the substitute of _pratipadika_ which ends with the word गो 'a cow,' and of that which ends with what has as its termination a feminine affix; when regarded as _upasarjana_.

[124] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.49)
SANSKRIT: तद्धितलुकि सति स्त्रीप्रत्ययस्य उपसर्जनस्य लुग्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When a _Taddhita_ affix is elided by the word _luk_, there takes place the elision by _luk_ of the feminine affix of the _upasarjana_.

[125] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.50)
SANSKRIT: गोण्यास्तद्धितलुकि सति इकारादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The short is substituted in the place of _goni_, when _Taddhita_ affix is elided by _luk_.

[126] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.51)
SANSKRIT: लुपीति लुप्संज्ञया लुप्ततस्य प्रत्ययस्यार्थ उच्यते; तत्र लुपि युक्तवद्व्यक्ति वचने भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: When a _Taddhita_ affix is elided by using the word लुप्, then the gender and number (of the derivative word) agree with those of the original word.

[127] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.52)
SANSKRIT: लुबर्थस्ययानि विशेषणानि तेषामपि व्यक्तिवचने भवतो जातिं वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: And of adjectives, (the gender and number are the same as of the word formed by _lup_ elision of the _Taddhita_ and which they qualify) so far as the _jati_ (or kind or species) is concerned; (or when not expressing _jati_.)

[128] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.53)
SANSKRIT: तदशिष्यं न वक्तव्यं कस्मात्संज्ञाप्रमाणत्वात् ॥
ENGLISH: This (concord of gender and number, of primitive and derivative nouns, and of attributes and substantives) need not be taught (or approved), because it has the authority of _samjna_ (or conventional term or idiom).

[129] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.54)
SANSKRIT: योऽयं जनपदे लुप् वरणादिभ्यश्चेति लुबुच्यते अयं न वक्तव्यः, किं कारणं योगाप्रख्यानात् नहि पञ्चाला वरणा इति योगः सम्बन्धः प्रख्यायते नैतदुपलभामहे बृक्षयोगान्नगरे वरणा इति ॥
ENGLISH: The _sutras_ declaring _lup_ elision need not also be taught, because of the non-currency of the etymological meaning of the words supposed to be formed by _lup_ elision.

[130] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.55)
SANSKRIT: पञ्चालादयः संज्ञा शब्दा न योगनिमित्ताइत्युक्तं तच्चावश्यमेवाभ्युपगन्तव्यं योगप्रमाणेहि तदभावेऽदर्शनं स्यात्, यदि पञ्चालादि शब्दो योगस्य प्रमाणं योगस्य वाचकः स्यात् ततस्तदभावेऽदर्शनमप्रयोगः स्यात् ॥
ENGLISH: And if the etymological meaning be held authoritative, then when such meaning is absent, the word also should vanish.

[131] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.56)
SANSKRIT: प्रधानं समासे किंचित्पदं प्रत्ययस्तव्यदादिः ताभ्यामर्थवचनमर्थाभिधानमनेन प्रकारेण भवतीति पूर्वाचार्य्यैः परिभाषितम् प्रधानोपसर्ज्जने प्रधानार्थेसहब्रूतः प्रकृति प्रत्ययौसहार्थे ब्रूत इति । तत् पाणिनिराचार्य्यः प्रत्याचष्टे अशिष्यमेतत् अर्थस्यान्यप्रमाणत्वादिति । अन्य इति शास्त्रापेक्षया लोकोव्यपदिश्यते, शब्दैरर्थाभिधानं स्वाभाविकं न पारिभाषिकमशक्यत्वात् । लोकत एवार्थावगतेः ॥
ENGLISH: (Nor need be taught) the rule relating to the dependence of the meaning of a word on the principal (_pradhana_, in a compound) or on the affix (_pratyaya_), because the authority of the meaning (of a word, compound or derivative) consists in something else.

[132] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.57)
SANSKRIT: कालोपसर्ज्जने चाशिष्ये कस्मादर्थस्यान्यप्रमाणत्वात् ॥
ENGLISH: And a rule fixing the meaning of Tense (_kala_) and _Upasarjana_ (sequence) is equally (unnecessary, and need not be taught).

[133] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.58)
SANSKRIT: जात्याख्यायामेकस्मिन्नर्थे बहुवचनमन्यतरस्यां भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In (a common) name (expressive of) class, in denoting the singular, the plural is optionally employed.

[134] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.59)
SANSKRIT: अस्मदो यो ऽर्थस्तस्यैकत्वे द्वित्वे च बहुवचनमन्यतरस्याम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The plural of the pronoun _asmad_, 'I,' is used optionally, though the sense requires a singular or dual number.

[135] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.60)
SANSKRIT: फल्गुन्योर्द्वयोः प्रोष्ठपदश्च द्वयोर्नक्षत्रयोर्बहुवचनमन्यतरस्याम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the dual of _Phalguni_ and _Proshthapada_, when signifying asterisms, (also connotes optionally plural).

[136] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.61)
SANSKRIT: द्वयोर्द्विवचने प्राप्ते पुनर्वस्वोश्छन्दसि विषये एकवचनमन्यतरस्यां भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the Vedas, the two stars, _Punarvasu_, may optionally be singular (and connote a dual).

[137] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.62)
SANSKRIT: द्विवचने प्राप्ते छन्दसि विषये विशाखयोरेकवचनमन्यतरस्यां भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the Vedas, the two stars _Visakha_ may optionally be in the singular number.

[138] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.63)
SANSKRIT: तिष्यपुनर्वस्वोर्नक्षत्रविषयेद्वन्द्वे बहुवचनप्रसङ्गे निन्यं द्विवचनं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the _Dvandva_ compounds of the stars _Tishya_ and _Punarvasu_, the dual constantly comes in the place of the plural.

[139] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.64)
SANSKRIT: सरूपाणां शब्दानां एकविभक्तौ परत एकशेषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the words having the same form, and all in the same one case-termination, the last one is only retained.

[140] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.65)
SANSKRIT: वृद्धोयूनासहवचनेशिष्यते युवानिवर्त्तते तल्लक्षणञ्चेदेव विशेषः ॥
ENGLISH: The _vriddha_ (or patronymic _Gotra_ word becomes _ekasesha_, and is retained, when compounded) with a patronymic word called _yuvan_, provided that the specific difference in form between them be in their signs (affix) only.

[141] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.66)
SANSKRIT: स्त्री वृद्धा यूना सहवचने शिष्यते तल्लक्षणश्चेदेव विशेषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And so also a feminine word, ending with a _vriddha_ affix, when similarly spoken along with the same word but which ends with a _yuvan_ affix, is only retained; and it is treated like a masculine.

[142] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.67)
SANSKRIT: स्त्रियासहवचने पुमान् शिष्यते स्वीनिवर्त्तते स्त्रपुंसलक्षणश्चेदेवविशेषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word in the masculine gender, similarly spoken along with the same word, but ending with the feminine affix, becomes _ekasesha_, and the latter is dropped.

[143] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.68)
SANSKRIT: यथासंख्यं भ्रातृपुत्रशाद्वौशिष्येते सहवचने स्वसृदुहितृभ्याम् ॥
ENGLISH: The words _bhratri_, 'brother,' and _putra_, 'son,' when spoken of along with _svasri_, 'sister' and _duhitri_ 'daughter' respectively are only retained, and the latter are dropped.

[144] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.69)
SANSKRIT: नपुंसकानपुंसकमात्रकृते विशेषेऽनपुंसकेन सहवचने नपुंसकशिष्यते; एकवच्चास्यकर्थे भवति अन्यतरस्याम् ॥
ENGLISH: A neuter noun, which has the same form, only differing in affix, is optionally retained, and the other is dropped, and it is like a singular number.

[145] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.70)
SANSKRIT: मात्रासह वचने पितृशब्दः शिष्यते अन्यतरस्याम् ॥
ENGLISH: The word _pitri_, 'father', is optionally only retained when spoken of along with _matri_, 'mother.'

[146] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.71)
SANSKRIT: श्वश्र्वासह वचने श्वशुरशब्दः शिष्यते अन्यतरस्याम् ॥
ENGLISH: The word _svasura_ 'father-in-law,' is optionally only retained, when spoken of along with _svasru_, 'mother-in-law.'

[147] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.72)
SANSKRIT: त्यदादीनि शब्दरूपाणि सर्वैः सहवचने नित्यं शिष्यन्ते त्यदाभिरन्यैश्च ॥
ENGLISH: The pronouns '_tyad_ &c.' when spoken of along with any other noun, (pronoun other than '_tyad_ &c.') are always retained as _ekasesha_, (to the exclusion of others).

[148] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.2.73)
SANSKRIT: ग्राम्याणां पशूनां संघाः ग्राम्यपशुसंघाः; एतेषु सह विवक्षायां स्त्री शिष्यते ॥
ENGLISH: The feminine noun is only retained, when denoting a collection of domestic animals, not being young.

[149] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.1)
SANSKRIT: भू इत्येवमादयः शब्दा क्रियावचना धातु संज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words beginning with _bhu_ 'to become,' and denoting action, are called _dhatu_ or verbal roots.

[150] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.2)
SANSKRIT: उपविश्यतेऽनेनेति उपदेशः, शास्त्रवाक्यानि सूत्रपाठः खिलपाठश्च तत्र योऽजनुनासिकः स इत्संज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The nasalized vowels are इत् in _Upadesa_, or original enunciation.

[151] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.3)
SANSKRIT: धात्वादेः समुदायस्य यदन्त्यं हल् तदित्संज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In _upadesa_, the final consonant of roots, &c. is इत्.

[152] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.4)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वेण प्राप्तायामित्संज्ञायां विभक्तौ वर्त्तमानानां तवर्ग सकार मकाराणां प्रतिषेध उच्यते ॥
ENGLISH: The final dental consonants, and the final स् and म् are not इत् in affixes called _vibhakti_ or inflective affixes.

[153] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.5)
SANSKRIT: ञि टु डु इत्येतेषां समुदायानामादितो वर्त्तमानानामित्संज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The initial _ni_, _tu_, and _du_ are इत् ॥

[154] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.6)
SANSKRIT: षकारः प्रत्ययस्यादिरित्संज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The initial ष् of an affix is indicatory.

[155] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.7)
SANSKRIT: चवर्ग टवर्गौ प्रत्ययस्यादी इत्संज्ञो भवतः॥
ENGLISH: The initial palatals and linguals of an affix are indicatory.

[156] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.8)
SANSKRIT: तद्धितवर्ज्जितस्य प्रत्ययस्यादितो वर्त्तमाना लकार शकार कवर्गा इत्संज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The initial _I_ and _s_, and the gutturals of all affixes, except _taddhita_, are indicatory.

[157] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.9)
SANSKRIT: तस्येत्संज्ञकस्य लोपो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of this, (namely of that which has been called इत्), there is elision.

[158] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.10)
SANSKRIT: समानां समसंख्यानां समपरिपठितानामुद्देशिनामनुदेशिनां च यथा क्रममुद्देशिभिरनुदेशिनः सम्बन्ध्यन्ते ॥
ENGLISH: When a rule involves the case of equal numbers of substitutes and of things for which these are to be substituted, their mutual correspondence or assignment of each to each, is according to the order of enumeration.

[159] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.11)
SANSKRIT: स्वरितो नाम स्वरविशेषो वर्णधर्म्मः तेन चिह्नेनाधिकारो वेदितव्यः ॥
ENGLISH: In these aphorisms, when a word is marked with a _svarita_ accent, by that an _adhikara_ or a governing rule is to be understood.

[160] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.12)
SANSKRIT: अनु्दात्तेतो ये धातवो ङितश्च तेभ्य एव आत्मने पदं भवति नान्येभ्यः ॥
ENGLISH: After a root which has an indicatory _anudatta_ vowel (_anudattet_) or an indicatory _n_ (_nit_), the affixes are those of the _Atmanepada_.

[161] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.13)
SANSKRIT: भावे कर्म्मणि चात्मनेपदम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An _atmanepada_ affix is the substitute of the affix ल (III. 4. 69) when it denotes the action of the verb or the object of the verb.

[162] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.14)
SANSKRIT: कर्म व्यतिहारे तद्विशिष्ट क्रिया वचनाद्धातोरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In denoting the agent, when reciprocity of action is to be expressed, the affixes of the _Atmanepada_ are employed.

[163] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.15)
SANSKRIT: गत्यर्थेभ्यो हिंसार्थेभ्यश्च धातुभ्यः कर्म व्यतिहारे आत्मनेपदं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After verbs having the sense of 'motion,' or 'injury,' when expressing interchange of action, the _Atmanepada_ affixes are not used.

[164] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.16)
SANSKRIT: इतरेतरोऽन्योन्य इत्येवमुपपदाद्धातोः कर्मव्यतिहारे आत्मनेपदं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And after the verbs which take the words _itaretara_ 'each other,' and _anyonya_ 'one another,' as _upapada_ (or dependant qualifying words), the affixes of _Atmanepada_ are not used, though reciprocity of action be denoted.

[165] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.17)
SANSKRIT: नेः परस्माद् विश आत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vis_ 'to enter,' when preceded by the preposition _ni_, the _Atmanepada_ affixes are employed.

[166] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.18)
SANSKRIT: परिव्यवेभ्य उत्तरात् क्रीणातेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ 'to purchase,' when preceded by _pari_, _vi_ or _ava_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[167] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.19)
SANSKRIT: वि परा पूर्वाज्जयतेर्धातोरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _ji_ 'to conquer,' preceded by _vi_ or _para_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed. This _sutra_ debars _Sutra_. (I. 2. 78) by which the root जि is generally Parasmaipadi.

[168] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.20)
SANSKRIT: आङ् पूर्वाद्ददातेरनास्यविहरणे वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _da_ 'to give,' preceded by _an_, and when not meaning 'to open the mouth,' the _Atmanepada_ affix is used, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[169] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.21)
SANSKRIT: क्रीडृ विहारे एतस्माद् अनुसम्परि इत्येवं पूर्वाद् आङ् पूर्वाच्चात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _krid_ 'to play' preceded by _anu_, _sam_ or _pari_, as well as _an_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is used.

[170] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.22)
SANSKRIT: सम् अव प्र वि इत्येवं पूर्वात् तिष्ठते रात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _stha_ 'to stand,' preceded by _sam_, _ava_, _pra_, _vi_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is used.

[171] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.23)
SANSKRIT: प्रकाशने स्थेयाख्यायां च तिष्ठतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _stha_ when meaning 'to indicate one's intentions to another,' or 'to make an award as an arbitrator,' the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed.

[172] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.24)
SANSKRIT: उत् पूर्वात् तिष्ठते रनूर्ध्वकर्म्मणि वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _stha_, preceded by _ut_, when not meaning 'to get up or rise,' as from a seat, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed.

[173] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.25)
SANSKRIT: उपपूर्वात्तिष्ठतेर्मन्त्रकरणेऽर्थेवर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _stha_, preceded by _upa_, when meaning 'to adore,' the _Atmanepada_ affix is used.

[174] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.26)
SANSKRIT: उपपूर्वात् तिष्ठतेरकर्मकादकर्मकक्रियावचनादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _stha_, preceded by _upa_, when used intransitively, the _Atmanepada_ affix is used.

[175] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.27)
SANSKRIT: उद् विइत्येवं पूर्वात् तपतेरकर्मकक्रियावचनादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _tapa_ 'to shine,' when used intransitively, and preceded by _ut_ or _vi_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed.

[176] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.28)
SANSKRIT: यम उपरमे, हन हिंसागत्योः । ताभ्यामकर्मकक्रिया वचनाभ्यामाङ् पूर्वाभ्यामात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _yam_ 'to stop,' and _han_ 'to injure' when used intransitively and preceded by _an_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed.

[177] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.29)
SANSKRIT: संपूर्वेभ्यो गमि ऋच्छि प्रच्छि स्वरति अर्तिश्रुविदिइत्येतेभ्योऽकर्मकेभ्यो धातुभ्यः आत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verbs _gam_ 'to go,' _richchh_ 'to become hard,' _prachchh_ 'to ask,' _svar_ 'to find fault,' _ri_ 'to go,' _sru_ 'to hear,' and _vid_ 'to know,' when used intransitively and preceded by _sam_, the _Atmanepada_ affix is employed.

[178] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.30)
SANSKRIT: नि सम उप वि इत्येवं पूर्वाद् ह्वयतेर्द्धातोरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verbs _hve_ 'to call,' preceded by _ni_, _sam_, _upa_, and _vi_, the _Atmanepada_ is used, even, when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[179] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.31)
SANSKRIT: स्पर्द्धायां विषये आङ् पूर्वाद् ह्वयतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _hve_, when meaning 'to challenge' and preceded by _an_, the _Atmanepada_ is used, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[180] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.32)
SANSKRIT: गन्धनादिष्वर्थेषु वर्त्तमानात् करोतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ when meaning 'to divulge,' 'to revile,' 'to serve,' 'to use violence,' 'to cause change,' 'to recite,' and 'to do an act tending to effect a desired purpose,' the _Atmanepada_ is used, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[181] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.33)
SANSKRIT: अधि पूर्वात् करोतेः प्रसहने वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ preceded by _adhi_, when the sense is that of 'overcoming or defeat,' the _Atmanepada_ is used, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[182] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.34)
SANSKRIT: वि पूर्वात् करोतेरकर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले शब्दकर्म्मण आत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ preceded by _vi_, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent, and when the sense is that of 'making sound,' (literally, having 'sound' for its object) the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[183] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.35)
SANSKRIT: वि पूर्वात् करोतेरकर्मकादकर्म्मकक्रियावचनादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ preceded by _vi_, when used intransitively, the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[184] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.36)
SANSKRIT: णीञ् प्रापणे इत्येतस्माद्धातोकर्त्रभिप्रायेक्रियाफलेरात्मनेपदं भवति, सम्माननादिषु विशेषणेषु सत्सु ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _ni_ 'to lead,' when used in the sense of 'to guide so as to render the person guided worthy,' 'to lift up,' 'to make one a spiritual guide,' 'to determine the true sense,' to employ on wages,' 'to pay as debt,' and 'to give as in charity,' even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent, the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[185] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.37)
SANSKRIT: नयतेः कर्त्तादेवदत्तादिर्लकारवाच्यः कर्तृस्थे कर्म्मण्यशरीरे सति नयतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _ni_, when it governs an incorporeal object existing in the agent, as its object, the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[186] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.38)
SANSKRIT: वृत्त्यादिष्वर्थेषु क्रमेर्धातोरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_ 'to move' when used, in the senses of 'continuity,' 'energy' and 'development,' the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[187] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.39)
SANSKRIT: उपपरापूर्वात् क्रमतेर्वृत्त्यादिष्वर्थेषु वर्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_, preceded by _upa_ and _para_, when used in the senses of continuity, energy and development, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[188] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.40)
SANSKRIT: आङ् पूर्वात् क्रमते रुद्गमने वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_, preceded by _an_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when used in the sense of the rising of a luminary.

[189] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.41)
SANSKRIT: विपूर्वात् क्रमतेः पादविहरणेऽर्थे वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_, preceded by _vi_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when used in the sense of 'placing of foot-steps.'

[190] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.42)
SANSKRIT: प्र उप इत्येताभ्यां परस्मात् क्रमतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed when it is preceded by _pra_ and _upa_, both conveying the same sense; viz. that of 'beginning an action.'

[191] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.43)
SANSKRIT: उपसर्ग वियुक्तात् क्रमतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kram_, the _Atmanepada_ is optionally employed, when it is not preceded by any preposition.

[192] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.44)
SANSKRIT: जानातेरपह्नवे वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _jna_, when used in the sense of 'denying,' the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[193] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.45)
SANSKRIT: जानातेरकर्मकादकर्मकक्रियावचनादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when used intransitively, after the verb _jna_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[194] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.46)
SANSKRIT: संप्रतिइत्येवं पूर्वाज्जानातेरनाध्याने वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _jna_, precede by _sam_, and _prati_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when not used in the sense of 'remembering with regret.'

[195] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.47)
SANSKRIT: भासनादिषु विशेषणेषु सत्सु वदतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vad_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when used in the senses of 'showing brilliance, or proficiency in,' 'pacifying,' 'knowledge,' 'effort,' 'difference of opinion,' and 'flattering.'

[196] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.48)
SANSKRIT: व्यक्तवाचां समुच्चारणं सहोचारणं तत्रवर्त्तमानाद्वदतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vad_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed when used in the sense of 'speaking articulately in a similar manner.'

[197] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.49)
SANSKRIT: अनुपूर्वाद्वदतेरकर्म्मदाद्व्यक्तवाग्विषयादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vad_, preceded by _anu_ when it is intransitively used, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when the sense is that of 'speaking articulately in a similar manner.'

[198] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.50)
SANSKRIT: विप्रलापात्मके व्यक्तवाचां समुच्चारणे वर्त्तमानाद्वदतेरात्मनेपदं भवति विभाषा ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vad_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed optionally, when the sense is that of 'contradicting each other.'

[199] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.51)
SANSKRIT: अवपूर्वाद् गिरतेरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _gri_ 'to swallow,' when preceded by _ava_, the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[200] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.52)
SANSKRIT: संपूर्वाद् गिरतेः प्रतिज्ञाने वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _gri_ preceded by _sam_, the _Atmanepada_ is used, when employed in the sense of 'promising.'

[201] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.53)
SANSKRIT: उत्पूर्वातूचरतेः सकर्म्मकक्रियावचनात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _char_ 'to walk' preceded by _ut_, when used transitively, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[202] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.54)
SANSKRIT: संपूर्वाच्चरतेस्तृतीयायुक्तादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _char_, preceded by _sam_, and connected with a noun in the Instrumental case, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[203] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.55)
SANSKRIT: दाणदाने परस्मैपदी ततः संपूर्वाद् तृतीयायुक्तादात्मनेपदं भवति साचेत् तृतीया चतुर्थ्यर्थे भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And after the verb _da_ to give, preceded by _sam_, and connected with a noun in the Instrumental case, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, provided this Instrumental case have the sense of the Dative case.

[204] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.56)
SANSKRIT: उपपूर्वाद्यमः स्वकरणे वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _yam_, to give, preceded by _upa_, when used in the sense of 'espousing,' the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[205] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.57)
SANSKRIT: ज्ञा श्रु स्मृ दृश् इत्येतेषां सन्नन्तानामात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the Desideratives formed by the affix _san_, of the verbs _jna_ to know, _sru_ to hear, _smri_ to remember, and _dris_ to see, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[206] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.58)
SANSKRIT: अनुपूर्वाज्जानातेः सन्नन्तादात्मनेपदं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the desiderative of _jna_ when preceded by _anu_ the _Atmanepada_ is not employed.

[207] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.59)
SANSKRIT: प्रति आङ् इत्येवं पूर्वाच्छृणोतेः सन्नन्तादात्मनेपदं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the desideratives of _sru_ when preceded by _prati_ and _an_ the _Atmanepada_ is not used.

[208] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.60)
SANSKRIT: शदिर्यः शिद्भावीशितो वा सम्बन्धी तस्मादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _sad_ to decay, when it has one of the affixes with an indicatory _s_ (_sit_) the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[209] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.61)
SANSKRIT: म्रियतेर्लुङ्लिङोः शितश्चात्मनेपदं भवति अन्यत्र न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _mri_ to die, when it has one of the affixes having an indicatory श, as well as when it takes the affixes _lun_ (aorist (III 2. 110)) add _lin_ (Benedictive (III. 3. 159)) the _Atmanepada_ is used.

[210] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.62)
SANSKRIT: सनः पूर्वो यो धातुः आत्मनेपदी तद्वत्सन्नन्तादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The verb which is _Atmanepadi_ in its primitive form before the taking of the affix _san_, will also be _Atmanepadi_ when it ends in the affix _san_. In other words; after a desiderative verb, _Atmanepada_ is employed, if it would have been used after the primitive verb.

[211] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.63)
SANSKRIT: आम्प्रत्ययस्थेवधातोः कृञोऽनुप्रयोगस्यात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Like the verb that takes the affix _am_, if the verb be conjugated with the _Atmanepada_ terminations, so of the verb _kri_ when subjoined thereto as an auxiliary, the terminations are of the _Atmanepada_, even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[212] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.64)
SANSKRIT: प्र उप इत्येवंपूर्वाद् युजेरयज्ञपात्रप्रयोगविषयादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _yuj_ to join the _Atmanepada_ is used, when it is preceded by _pra_ and _upa_ except with reference to sacrificial vessels even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[213] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.65)
SANSKRIT: क्ष्णु तेजने परस्मैपदी ततः संपूर्वादात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kshnu_, to sharpen, preceded by _sam_, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[214] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.66)
SANSKRIT: भुजपालनाभ्यवहारयोरिति रुधादौ पठ्यते । तस्मादनवनेऽपालने वर्त्तमानादात्मने पदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _bhuj_, the _Atmanepada_ is used, except in the sense of protecting.

[215] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.67)
SANSKRIT: ण्यन्तादात्मनेपदं भवति, कथम् अणौ यत्कर्म णौ चेत् तदेवकर्म, स एव कर्त्ता भवति । अनाध्याने, आध्यानं वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: After a verb ending in the affix _ni_ (causal) the _Atmanepada_ is employed, provided that when the object in the non-_ni_ or non-causal sense becomes the agent in the causal; and when it does not mean 'to remember with regret;' even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[216] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.68)
SANSKRIT: बिभेतेः स्मयतेश्वण्यन्तादात्मनेपदं भवति हेतुभये ॥
ENGLISH: After the causatives of the verbs _bhi_ to fear, and _smi_ to wonder, even the fruit of the action accrues not to the agent the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when the fear is produced directly by the causative agent.

[217] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.69)
SANSKRIT: गृधु अभिकाङ्क्षायाम् वञ्चु गतावित्येतयोर्ण्यन्तयोः प्रलम्भने वर्त्तमानयोरात्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the causatives of the verbs _gridh_ to covet, and _vanch_ to go, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when used in the sense of deceiving, even though the fruit of action does not accrue to the agent.

[218] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.70)
SANSKRIT: लियो ण्यन्तात्संमानने शालीनीकरणे च वर्त्तमानादात्मनेपदं भवति च शब्दात् प्रलम्भने च ॥
ENGLISH: After the causative of the verb _li_ to melt or stick, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when used in the sense of showing respect, subduing and deceiving, even though the fruit of action does not accrue to the agent.

[219] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.71)
SANSKRIT: ण्यन्तात् करोतेर्मिथ्योपपदादात्मनेपदं भवति अभ्यासे ॥
ENGLISH: After the causative of the verb _kri_, the _Atmanepada_ is used, when it has the word _mithya_, incorrect, as an _upapada_ or dependent word, and is employed in the sense of 'repeated wrong utterance' even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

[220] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.72)
SANSKRIT: स्वरितेतो ये धातवो ञितश्च तेभ्य आत्मनेपदं भवति कर्त्तारं चेत् क्रियाफलमभिप्रैति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb marked with a _svarita_, (_svaritet_) or which has an indicatory _n_ (_nit_), the terminations of the _Atmanepada_ are employed, when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[221] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.73)
SANSKRIT: अपपूर्वाद्वदतेः कर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले आत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vad_ to tell, preceded by _apa_, when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent, the terminations are of the _Atmanepada_.

[222] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.74)
SANSKRIT: णिजन्तादात्मनेपदं भवति कर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले ॥
ENGLISH: After a verb ending in affix _ni_ (causal) when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent, the _Atmanepada_ is employed.

[223] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.75)
SANSKRIT: सम् उद् आङ् इत्येवं पूर्वाद्यमेः कर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले आत्मनेपदं भवति ग्रन्थविषयश्चेत्प्रयोगो न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _yam_ to strive, preceded by _sam_, _ut_ and _an_, when it does not refer to a book, the _Atmanepada_ is employed, when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[224] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.76)
SANSKRIT: अनुपसर्गाज्जानातेः कर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले आत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _jna_ when not preceded by any _upasarga_, the terminations are of the _Atmanepada_, when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[225] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.77)
SANSKRIT: समीपेश्रूयमाण शब्दान्तरमुपपदं तेन प्रतीयमाने कर्त्रभिप्राये क्रियाफले विभाषाऽत्मनेपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _Atmanepada_ is optionally used, when the fact of the fruit of the action accuring to the agent is indicated by an _upapada_, i.e. by a word used along with the verb.

[226] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.78)
SANSKRIT: शेषात्कर्तरिपरस्मैपदं भवति शेषादेवनान्यस्मात् अनुदात्तङित आत्मनेपदमुक्तम् ॥
ENGLISH: After the rest, i.e. after all those verbs not falling under any one of the previous provisions, the terminations of the _parasmaipada_ are employed in marking the agent (i.e. in the active voice).

[227] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.79)
SANSKRIT: अनुपरा इत्येवं पूर्वात् करोतेः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kri_ to make, preceded by _anu_ and _para_, _parasmaipada_ is employed, even when the fruit of the action goes to the agent, and when the sense is that of 'divulging,' &c.

[228] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.80)
SANSKRIT: अभिप्रति अतिइत्येवं पूर्वात् क्षिपः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _kship_, to throw, coming after _abhi_, _prati_ and _ati_, _parasmaipada_ is used, even though the fruit of the action goes to the agent.

[229] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.81)
SANSKRIT: प्र पूर्वाद् वहतेः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _vah_ to bear, coming after _pra_, _parasmaipada_ is used, even though the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[230] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.82)
SANSKRIT: परिपूर्वाद् मृष्यतेः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _mrish_ to bear, preceded by _pari_, _parasmaipada_ is used, even when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[231] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.83)
SANSKRIT: विआङ्परिइत्येवं पूर्वाद्रमतेः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _ram_ to sport, preceded by _vi_ and an, _parasmaipada_ is used.

[232] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.84)
SANSKRIT: उपपूर्वाद्रमतेः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And also after _upa_, the verb _ram_ takes the affixes of the _parasmaipada_.

[233] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.85)
SANSKRIT: उपपूर्वाद्रमतेरकर्मकाद्विभाषा परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _ram_ preceded by _upa_, _parasmaipada_ is optionally used, when employed intransitively.

[234] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.86)
SANSKRIT: बुधयुधनशजन एङ् प्रु द्रु स्रु इत्येतेभ्योण्यन्तेभ्यः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verbs _budh_ to know, _yudh_ to fight, _nas_ to destroy, _jana_ to be born, _in_ to go, _pru_ to move, _dru_ to run, and _sru_ to flow, ending in the affix _ni_ (i.e., when used in the causative), _parasmaipada_ is employed, even when the fruit of the action goes to the agent.

[235] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.87)
SANSKRIT: निगरणार्थेभ्यश्च चलनार्थेभ्यश्च धातुभ्योण्यन्तेभ्यः परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And after the causatives of verbs which have the sense of the 'eating or swallowing' and 'shaking or moving' _parasmaipada_ is employed, even when the fruit of the action goes to the agent.

[236] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.88)
SANSKRIT: अण्यन्तो यो धातुरकर्मकश्चित्तवत्कर्तृकश्चतस्माद्ण्यन्तात् परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes of the _parasmaipada_ are employed after the causal of that verb, which in its non-causal state was intransitive and had a being endowed with reason for its agent; even when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

[237] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.89)
SANSKRIT: पादमि आङ्यम आङ्यस परिमुह रुचि नृति वदवसः इत्येतेभ्योण्यन्तेभ्यः परस्मैपदं न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: But the affixes of the _parasmaipada_ are not used after the causals of the verbs _pa_ to drink, _dam_ to tame, _ayam_ to extend, _ayas_ to exert oneself, _parimuh_ to be bewildered, _ruch_ to shine, _nrit_ to dance, _vad_ to speak, and _vas_ to dwell.

[238] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.90)
SANSKRIT: क्यषन्ताद्धातोर्वा परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The affixes of the _parasmaipada_ are used optionally after the denominative verbs ending in the affix _kyash_.

[239] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.91)
SANSKRIT: द्युतादिभ्यो लुङि वा परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verbs _dyut_ to shine, &c. the terminations of the _parasmaipada_ are optionally employed, when the affixes of _lun_ (aorist) follow.

[240] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.92)
SANSKRIT: वृतु, वृधु शधु स्यन्द्क्तपू । एतेभ्यो धातुभ्यः स्येसनि च परतो परस्मैपदं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verbs _vrit_ to exist, &c., _Parasmaipada_ is optionally employed when the affixes _sya_(Future and conditional) and _san_ (Desiderative) follow.

[241] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.3.93)
SANSKRIT: लुटि च स्यसनोश्च कृपे परस्मैपदं वा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _klip_ to be fit, _Parasmaipada_ is optionally employed, when _lut_ (Ist Future) is affixed, as well as when _sya_ and _san_ are affixed.

[242] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.1)
SANSKRIT: कडाराः कर्मधारय इति वक्ष्यति आ एतस्मात् सूत्रावधेर्यदित उर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामः तत्र एकासंज्ञाभवतीति वेदितव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: From this _sutra_ up to the aphorism _Kadarah_ _Karmadharaye_ (II. 2. 38) only one name of each thing named is to be understood.

[243] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.2)
SANSKRIT: (तुल्यबलविरोधो विप्रतिषेधः) तस्मिन् विप्रतिषेधे परंकार्यम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When rules of equal force prohibit each other, then the last in the order herein given is to take effect.

[244] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.3)
SANSKRIT: ईकारान्तमूकारान्तं च स्त्र्याख्यं शब्दरूपं नदी संज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Word-forms ending in long _i_ and _u_ being names of females are called _Nadi_.

[245] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.4)
SANSKRIT: इयङ् उवङोः स्थान मनयोरितीयङुवङ्स्थानौ तौ यू नदीसंज्ञौ न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: Feminine words ending in _i_ and _u_ which admit the substitute (इयङ्) _iyan_ and (उवङ्) _uvan_ (VI. 4. 77) are not called _Nadi_; except the word _stri_, (which is called _nadi_) notwithstanding its substituting _iyan_.

[246] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.5)
SANSKRIT: इयङुवङ्स्थानौ यू आमिपरतो वा नदीसंज्ञो न भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: Feminine words ending in _i_ and _u_, though admitting _iyan_ and _uvan_ substitutes, are optionally termed _Nadi_, when the affix _am_ (Gen Pl.) follows, but not so the word _stri_, which is always _Nadi_.

[247] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.6)
SANSKRIT: ङिति परतो हृस्वश्च य्वोः सम्बन्धी यः स्त्र्याख्यौ इयङुवङस्थानौ च यू वा नदीसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: When a case-affix having an indicatory _n_ (_nit_) follows, then feminine words ending in short _i_ and _u_ are optionally termed _Nadi_, as well as feminine nouns in long _i_ and _u_ which admit of _iyan_ and _uvan_; but not so the word _stri_, which is always _Nadi_.

[248] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.7)
SANSKRIT: शेषो ऽत्र घिसंज्ञो भवति सखिशब्दं वर्जयित्वा । क श्च शेषः ? हृस्वमिवर्णोवर्णान्तंयन्नस्त्र्याख्यं, स्त्र्याख्यं च यन्ननदीसंज्ञकं स शेषः ॥
ENGLISH: The rest of the words that end in short इ and उ are called _ghi_ with the exception of the word _sakhi_.

[249] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.8)
SANSKRIT: पतिशब्दः समास एव घिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _pati_ is called _ghi_ only when it is in a compound.

[250] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.9)
SANSKRIT: षष्ठ्यन्तेन युक्तः पतिशब्दः छन्दसि विषये वा, घिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _pati_ when used in connection with a noun ending in the sixth or genitive case, is _ghi_, optionally, in the _Chhandas_ (_veda_).

[251] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.10)
SANSKRIT: हृस्वमक्षरं लघुसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A short vowel is called 'light' (_laghu_).

[252] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.11)
SANSKRIT: संयोगे परतो हृस्वमक्षरं गुरुसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When a conjunct consonant follows, a short vowel is termed 'heavy' (_Guru_).

[253] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.12)
SANSKRIT: दीर्घचाक्षरंगुरुसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And a long vowel is also termed heavy, (_Guru_).

[254] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.13)
SANSKRIT: यस्मात् प्रत्ययो विधीयते धातोर्वा प्रातिपदिकाद्वा तदादि शब्दरूपं प्रत्यये परतोऽङ्गसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After whatsoever there is an affix enjoined, whether verbal root or crude-form, that which begins therewith in the form in which it appears when the affix follows it, is called an Inflective base (_anga_).

[255] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.14)
SANSKRIT: सुबन्तं तिङन्तं च शब्दरूपं पदसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which ends in _sup_ (case-affix) (IV. 1. 2); or in _tin_ (III. 4. 78) (tense-affix), is called a _pada_ or inflected word.

[256] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.15)
SANSKRIT: नान्तं शब्दरूपं क्ये परतः पदसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word-form ending in _n_, is called _pada_, when _kya_ follows (i.e., the affixes _kyach_, _kyan_ and _kyash_).

[257] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.16)
SANSKRIT: सिति प्रत्यये परतः पूर्वं पदसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When an affix having an indicatory स् follows then that which precedes it is called _pada_.

[258] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.17)
SANSKRIT: स्वादिषु प्रत्ययेषु परतः सर्वनामस्थानवर्जितेषु पूर्वं पदसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the afflxes beginning with सु (IV. 1. 2) and ending in कप् (V. 4. 151). follow, not being _Sarvanamasthana_ (I. 1. 43) then that which precedes is called _pada_.

[259] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.18)
SANSKRIT: यकारादाधजादौ च स्वादौ सर्वनामस्थानवर्जिते प्रत्यये परतः पूर्वं भसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when an affix, with an initial _y_ or an initial vowel, being one of the affixes, beginning with _su_ and ending in _k_, follows, not being _Sarvanamasthana_, then what precedes, is called _Bha_.

[260] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.19)
SANSKRIT: तकारान्तं सकारान्तं शब्दरूपं मत्वर्थे प्रत्यये परतो भसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word-form ending in _t_ or in _s_ is called _Bha_ when an affix with the force of _matup_ ('whose is it,' 'or in whom it is' (V. 2. 94)) follows.

[261] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.20)
SANSKRIT: अयस्मयादीनि शब्दरूपाणि च्छन्दसि विषये साधूनि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: Words like _ayasmaya_ &c., are valid forms in the _chhandas_ (_veda_).

[262] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.21)
SANSKRIT: बहुषु बहुवचनम् भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In expressing multeity, a Plural case affix is employed.

[263] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.22)
SANSKRIT: द्वित्वैकत्वयोरर्थयोर्द्विवचनैकवचने भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The dual and singular case-affixes are employed severally in the sense of duality and unity.

[264] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.23)
SANSKRIT: कारक इत्यधिकारो वेदितव्यः, यदित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामः कारक इत्येवं तद्वेदितव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: The phrase '_karaka_' (meaning 'in the special relation to a word expressing an action') is to be understood in the following aphorisms.

[265] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.24)
SANSKRIT: ध्रुवं यदपाययुक्त नपाये साध्ये यदवधिभूतं तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A noun whose relation to an action is that of a fixed point from which departure takes place is called _apadana_ or Tablation.

[266] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.25)
SANSKRIT: बिभेत्यर्थानां त्रायत्यर्थानां च धातूनां प्रयोगे भयहेतुर्यस्तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of words implying 'fear' and 'protection from danger' that from which the danger or fear procedes is called _Apadana_ _karaka_.

[267] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.26)
SANSKRIT: परापूर्वस्य जयतेः प्रयोगेऽसोढो योऽर्थः सोढुं नशक्यते तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verb _paraji_, 'to be tired or weary of,' that which becomes unbearable, is called _Apadana_ _karaka_.

[268] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.27)
SANSKRIT: वारणार्थानां धातूनां प्रयोगे य ईप्सितोऽर्थः तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of verbs having the sense of 'preventing', the desired object from which one is prevented or warded off is called Ablation or _Apadana_ _karaka_.

[269] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.28)
SANSKRIT: अन्तर्द्धिनिमित्तं येनादर्शनमात्मनइच्छतितत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When concealment is indicated, the person whose sight one wishes to avoid, is called _Apadana_ _karaka_.

[270] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.29)
SANSKRIT: उपयोगे साध्ये य आख्याता तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The noun denoting the teacher is called _Apadana_ or ablation, in relation to the action signifying formal teaching.

[271] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.30)
SANSKRIT: जन्यर्थस्यजन्मनः कर्त्ता जायमानः तस्यया प्रकृतिः कारणं हेतुः तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The prime cause of the agent of the verb _jan_ to be born, is called _Apadana_.

[272] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.31)
SANSKRIT: भूकर्त्तुः प्रभवो य स्तत्कारकमपादानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The source of the agent of the verb _bhu_, to become, is called _Apadana_.

[273] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.32)
SANSKRIT: कर्मणा, करणभूतेन कर्त्ता यमभिप्रैति तत्कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The person whom one wishes to connect with the object of giving, is called _Sampradana_ or recipient.

[274] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.33)
SANSKRIT: रुच्यर्थानां धातूनां प्रयोगे प्रीयमाणो योऽर्थ स्तत्कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In case of verbs having the signification of the root _ruch_ 'to like,' the person or thing that is pleased or satisfied, is called _Sampradana_ or recipient.

[275] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.34)
SANSKRIT: श्लाघ न्हुङ् स्था शप् इत्येतेषां ज्ञीप्स्यमानो योऽर्थ स्तत् कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of verbs _slagh_ to praise, _hnu_ to take away _stha_ to stand, and _sap_ to curse, the person whom it is intended to inform of or persuade by, these actions, is called _Sampradana_.

[276] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.35)
SANSKRIT: धारयतेः प्रयोगे उत्तमर्णौ योऽर्थ स्तत्कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verb _dhari_ 'to owe,' the creditor is called _Sampradana_.

[277] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.36)
SANSKRIT: स्पृहे ईप्सायां, चुरादावदन्तः पठ्यते, तस्य इप्सितो योऽर्थस्तत्कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verb _sprih_ to desire, the thing desired is called _Sampradana_ _karaka_.

[278] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.37)
SANSKRIT: क्रुधाद्यर्थानां प्रयोगे यं प्रतिकोपस्तत्कारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verbs having the sense of _krudh_ to be angry, _druh_ to injure, _irshya_ to envy, _asuya_ to detract, the person against whom the feeling of anger &c., is directed is called _Sampradana_.

[279] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.38)
SANSKRIT: क्रुधद्रुहो रुपसृष्टयो रुपसर्गसंबन्धयो र्यं प्रतिकोपस्तत्कारकं कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: But in the case of the verbs _krudh_ and _druh_, when preceded by prepositions, the person against whom the feeling of anger &c., is directed is called _karma_ _karaka_ or object.

[280] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.39)
SANSKRIT: राधेरीक्षेश्चकारकं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति कीदृशं यस्य विप्रश्नः, विविधः प्रश्नः, विप्रश्नः स कस्य भवति यस्य शुभाशुभं पृछ्यते ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verbs _radh_, to propitiate, and _iksh_ to look to, the person about whose good or bad fortune questions are asked is called _Sampradana_.

[281] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.41)
SANSKRIT: अनुपूर्वस्य प्रतिपूर्वस्य च शृणातेः कारकं पूर्वस्याः क्रियायाः कृत्तृभूतं संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of the verb _gri_, preceded by _anu_ and _prati_, and meaning 'to encourage by repeating', the person who was the agent of the prior action, which is repeated, is called _Sampradana_.

[282] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.42)
SANSKRIT: क्रियाप्रसिद्धौ यत् प्रकृष्टोपकारकं विवक्षितं तत्साधकतमं कारकं करणसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which is especially auxiliary in the accomplishment of the action is called the Instrument or _karana_ _karaka_.

[283] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.43)
SANSKRIT: दिवः साधकतमं यत्कारकं तत्कर्मसंज्ञं भवति चकारात् करणसंज्ञं च ॥
ENGLISH: That which is especially auxiliary in the accomplishment of the action, of the verb _div_ to play, is called _karma_ object, as well as _karana_, Instrument.

[284] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.44)
SANSKRIT: परिक्रयणे साधकतमं कारकमन्यतरस्यां संप्रदानसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of hiring on wages, that which is especially auxiliary in the accomplishment of the action of the verb _parikri_, 'employing on stipulated wages,' is optionally called _Sampradana_ or recipient.

[285] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.45)
SANSKRIT: कर्त्तृकर्मणोः क्रियाश्रयभूतयोर्धारण क्रियां प्रति य आधारस्तत्कारकमधिकरणसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: (_ADHIKARANA_). That which is related to the action as the site where the action is performed by reason of the agent or the object being in that place is called _Adhikarana_ or the Location.

[286] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.46)
SANSKRIT: अधिपूर्वाणां शीङ् स्था आस् इत्येतेषा माधारोयस्तत्कारकं कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which is the site of the verbs _si_ to lie down, _stha_, to stand, _as_ to sit, when preceded by the preposition _adhi_, is however called _karma_ _karaka_ or object.

[287] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.47)
SANSKRIT: अभिनिपूर्वस्य विशतेराधारो यस्तत्कारकं कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which is the site of the verb _abhinivis_ to enter, is also called _karma_-_karaka_.

[288] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.48)
SANSKRIT: उप अनु अधि आङ् इत्येव पूर्वस्य वसतेराधारो यस्तत्कारकं कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: That which is the site of the verb _vas_ to dwell, when preceded by _upa_, _anu_, _adhi_, and _an_, is called _karma_-_karaka_.

[289] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.49)
SANSKRIT: कर्त्तुः क्रियया यदाप्तुमिष्टतमं तत्कारकं कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: (_KARMA_). That which it is intended should be most affected by the of the agent is called the object or _karma_.

[290] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.50)
SANSKRIT: येन प्रकारेण कर्त्तु रीप्सिततमं क्रियया युज्यते तेनैव चेत् प्रकारेण यदनीप्सितं युक्तं भवति तस्य कर्मसंज्ञा विधीयते ॥
ENGLISH: If that which is not intended to be most affected by the act becomes however similarly connected with the action it also is called _karma_.

[291] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.51)
SANSKRIT: अकथितं च यत्कारकं तत्कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And that _karaka_ which is not spoken of as coming under any of the special relations of ablation &c., is also called _karma_.

[292] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.52)
SANSKRIT: गत्यर्थानां बुद्ध्यर्थानां प्रत्यवसानार्थानां च धातूनां तथाशब्दकर्मकाणामकर्मकाणाञ्च अण्यन्तानां यः कर्त्ता स ण्यन्तानां कर्मसंज्ञा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the verbs having the sense of 'motion' 'knowledge or information' and 'eating,' and of verbs that have some literary work for their object, and of intransitive verbs that which was the agent of the verb in its primitive (non-_ni_ or non-causal state), is called the object (_karma_) in its causative state (when the verb takes the affix (_ni_).

[293] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.53)
SANSKRIT: हरतेः करोतेश्चाण्यन्तयोर्यः कर्त्ता स ण्यन्तयो रन्यतरस्यां कर्मसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The agent of the verb in its non-_ni_ (primitive) form in the case of _hri¬_ to lose and _kri_ to make, is optionally called _karma_ or object when these verbs take the affix.

[294] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.54)
SANSKRIT: क्रिया प्रसिद्धौ स्वातन्त्र्येण विवक्ष्यते तत्कारकं कर्तृसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: (_KARTRI_). Whatever the speaker chooses as the independent, principal and absolute source of action is called _karta_ or agent.

[295] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.55)
SANSKRIT: स्वतन्त्रस्य प्रयोजको योऽर्थः तत्कारकं हेतुसंज्ञं भवति चकारात् कर्तृसंज्ञं च ॥
ENGLISH: That which is the mover thereof, i. e., of the independent source of action, is called _Hetu_ or cause, as well as _karta_ or agent

[296] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.56)
SANSKRIT: अधिरीश्वर इति वक्ष्यति प्रागेतस्मादवधेर्यानित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामः निपात संज्ञा स्ते वेदितव्याः ॥
ENGLISH: (_Nipata_. THE PARTICLES). From this point forward upto the aphorism _Adhiri_-_svare_ (I 4. 97), all that we shall say is to be understood to have the name of _Nipata_ or Particles.

[297] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.57)
SANSKRIT: चादयो निपातसंज्ञा भवन्ति न चेत्सत्त्वे वर्त्तन्ते ॥
ENGLISH: The word _cha_ 'and,' &c., are called _Nipata_ or Particles, when they do not signify substances.

[298] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.58)
SANSKRIT: प्रादयो ऽसत्वे निपातसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _pra_ &c., are called _Nipata_ when not signifying substances.

[299] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.59)
SANSKRIT: प्रादयः क्रिया योगे उपसर्ग संज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _pra_ &c., get the designation of _upasarga_ or prepositions, when in composition with a verb.

[300] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.60)
SANSKRIT: गतिसंज्ञकाश्च प्रादयो भवन्ति क्रियायोगे ॥
ENGLISH: The words _pra_ &c., are called also _Gati_, when in composition with a verb.

[301] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.61)
SANSKRIT: ऊर्यादयः शब्दाः च्व्यन्ता डाजन्ताश्च क्रियायोगे गतिसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _uri_, assent, &c., and those that end with _chvi_ (V. 4. 50), and those that end with _dach_ (V. 4. 57), (when in composition with the verb _bhu_, _kri_ or _as_) are called _Gati_.

[302] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.62)
SANSKRIT: अनुकरणमनिति परम् क्रियायोगे गतिसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word imitative of sounds is also called _Gati_, when it is not followed by the word _iti_.

[303] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.63)
SANSKRIT: आदरा नादरयोर्यथाक्रमं सदसच्छब्दौ गतिसंज्ञो भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _sat_ and _asat_ when in composition with a verb are called _gati_, when used in the sense of 'respect or love,' and 'disrespect or indifference.'

[304] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.64)
SANSKRIT: भूषणे योऽलम् शब्दः स गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _alam_ when in composition with a verb is called _gati_, when used in the sense of 'ornament.'

[305] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.65)
SANSKRIT: अन्तः शब्दोऽपरिग्रहेऽर्थेगतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _antar_ is called _gati_, when used in the sense of 'non-accepting,' in composition with a verb.

[306] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.66)
SANSKRIT: कणेशब्दो मनस्शब्दश्च श्रद्धा प्रतीघाते गतिसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _kane_ and _manas_ are _gati_ when in composition with a verb and used in the sense of reaction by satiation.

[307] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.67)
SANSKRIT: असिप्रत्ययान्तः पुरः शब्दोऽव्ययं गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _purah_ in front of, when indeclinable, and in composition with a verb, is called _gati_.

[308] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.68)
SANSKRIT: अस्तं शब्दो मकारान्तोऽव्ययमनुपलब्धौ वर्तते स गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the indeclinable word _astam_ 'at home,' is called _gati_, when in composition with a verb.

[309] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.69)
SANSKRIT: अच्छशब्दोऽव्ययनभिशब्दस्यार्थे वर्तते, स गत्यर्थेषु धातुषु यदतौ च गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _achchha_, meaning 'before in the presence of,' is called _gati_, when used in composition with verbs denoting 'motion' or with the verb '_vad_' to speak.

[310] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.70)
SANSKRIT: अदः शब्दोऽनुपदेशे गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _adas_ 'that' is called _gati_ when in composition with a verb and not implying a direction to another.

[311] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.71)
SANSKRIT: अन्तर्द्धिर्व्यवधानम्, तत्रतिरः शब्दो गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
