ENGLISH: The word _tiras_ when used in the sense of 'disappearance,' is called _gati_ when in composition with a verb.

[312] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.72)
SANSKRIT: तिरः शब्दः करोतौ परतो विभाषा गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _tiras_ meaning 'disappearance' is optionally called _gati_, when the verb _kri_ follows.

[313] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.73)
SANSKRIT: उपाजेऽन्वाजेशब्दौ विभक्ति प्रतिरूपकौ निपातौ दुर्बलस्य सामर्थ्याधाने वर्तेते तौ कृञि विभाषा गतिसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _upaje_ and _anvaje_ both meaning 'supporting or assisting the weak,' are optionally called _gati_ when used along with the verb _kri_.

[314] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.74)
SANSKRIT: साक्षात्प्रभृतीनि शब्दरूपाणि कृञि विभाषा गतिसंज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _sakshat_, 'in the presence of' &c., are optionally called _gati_, when used along with the verb _kri_.

[315] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.75)
SANSKRIT: अनत्याधानमुपश्लेषणं तदभावेऽनत्याधाने उरसिमनसी शब्दौ विभाषा कृञिगतिसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The word _urasi_, 'in the breast,' and _manasi_ 'in the mind' are optionally _gati_ when the verb _kri_ follows, provided that they are not used in the sense of 'placing.'

[316] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.76)
SANSKRIT: मध्ये पदे निवचने इत्येते शब्दा, अनत्याधाने विभाषा कृञि गतिसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: And the words _madhye_ 'in the middle,' _pade_ 'in the foot' and _nivachane_ 'speechless' are optionally _gati_, when _kri_ follows, the sense not being of 'placing.'

[317] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.77)
SANSKRIT: हस्ते पाणौ इत्येतौ शब्दौ कृञि नित्यं गतिसंज्ञौ भवत उपयमने ॥
ENGLISH: The word _haste_ 'in the hand,' _panau_ 'in the hand' are always and necessarily called _gati_ when used with the verb _kri_ in the sense of 'marriage.'

[318] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.78)
SANSKRIT: प्राध्वमिति मकारान्तमव्ययमानुकूल्ये वर्त्तते, तदानुकूल्यं बन्धनहेतुकं यदा भवति तदाप्राध्वंशब्दः कृञि नित्यं गतिसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _pradhvam_ followed by the verb _kri_, is always called _gati_ when used in the sense of 'binding.'

[319] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.79)
SANSKRIT: जीविका उपनिषदित्येतौ शब्दौ औपम्ये विषये कृञि गतिसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _Jivika_ and _upanishad_ followed by the verb _kri_ are called _gati_ when used in the sense 'of likeness or resemblance.'

[320] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.81)
SANSKRIT: छन्दसि विषये गत्युपसर्ग संज्ञका; परेऽपि पूर्वेपि प्रयोक्तव्याः ॥
ENGLISH: In the _chhandas_ (_veda_) these _gati_ and _upasarga_ are employed indifferently after the verbal root as well as before it.

[321] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.82)
SANSKRIT: व्यवहिताश्च गत्युपसर्गसंज्ञकाः छन्दसि दृश्यन्ते ॥
ENGLISH: In the _chhandas_ (_veda_) these _gati_ and _upasarga_ are also seen separated from the verb by intervening words.

[322] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.83)
SANSKRIT: यानित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामः कर्मप्रवचनीय संज्ञास्ते वेदितव्यः ॥
ENGLISH: (_KARMA PRAVACHANIYA_). From this point as far as aphorism (I. 4. 97) the particles treated of, are to be understood as having the name of _karma_-_pravachaniya_.

[323] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.84)
SANSKRIT: अनुशब्दो लक्षणे द्योत्ये कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _anu_ when it denotes a sign, is called _karma_-_pravachaniya_.

[324] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.85)
SANSKRIT: अनुशब्दस्तृतीयार्थे द्योत्ये कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _anu_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when it has the force of the third case.

[325] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.86)
SANSKRIT: हीने द्योत्येऽयमनुः कर्मप्रवचनीय संज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _anu_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when it is used in the sense of inferior or subordinate to.

[326] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.87)
SANSKRIT: उपशब्दः अधिके हीने च द्योत्ये कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _upa_ when it means 'superior' or 'inferior to' is _karma_-_pravachaniya_.

[327] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.88)
SANSKRIT: अप परी शब्दौ वर्जनेद्योत्ये कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _apa_ and _pari_ are _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when meaning 'exclusion.'

[328] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.89)
SANSKRIT: आङ् इत्येषशब्दः मर्यादावचने कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _an_ as far as, is _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when it expresses limit (e. g. when it means 'as far as inclusive of' or, 'as far as exclusive of.')

[329] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.90)
SANSKRIT: लक्षणे इत्थंभूताख्याने भागे वीप्सायां च विषयभूतायां प्रति परि अनु इत्येते कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _prati_, _pari_ and _anu_ are _karma_ _pravachaniya_ when used in the sense of 'sign' (in the direction of) 'mere statement of circumstance' ('as regards') 'division' 'share of' and 'pervasion' severally.

[330] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.91)
SANSKRIT: लक्षणादिष्वेव भागवर्जितेऽवभिः कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _abhi_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_, in the above senses of 'in the direction of,' 'as regards,' and 'each severally' but not when it means division, 'share of.'

[331] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.92)
SANSKRIT: प्रतिनिधिविषये प्रातिदाने विषये च प्रतिः कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _prati_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when used in the sense of representative ('representative of') or exchange ('in exchange for.')

[332] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.93)
SANSKRIT: अधि परी शब्दौ अनर्थकौ अनर्थान्तरवाचिनौ कर्मप्रवचनीय संज्ञौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _adhi_ and _pari_ are _karma_-_pravachaniya_ when used as mere expletives.

[333] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.94)
SANSKRIT: सु शब्दः पूजायामर्थे कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _su_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_ in the sense of respect, (when it means 'excellently').

[334] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.95)
SANSKRIT: अति शब्दः अतिक्रमणे चकारात् पूजायांच कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _ati_ in the sense of super-abundance ('excessively') and 'excellently' is _karma_ _pravachaniya_.

[335] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.96)
SANSKRIT: पदार्थे संभावनेऽन्ववसर्गे गर्हायां समुच्चये च वर्तमानः अपिः कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _api_ is _karma_-_pravachaniya_, when it implies, the sense of word understood ('somewhat') or possibility (e. g., 'even' in the sense of such a great person), or permission to do as one likes, ('if you like'), or censure ('even' in the sense of what is disgraceful); or collection ('and').

[336] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.97)
SANSKRIT: ईश्वरः स्वामी सच स्वमपेक्षत । तदयं स्वस्वामि संबन्धः कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _adhi_ is _karmapravachaniya_ when used in the sense of 'lord' ('being as a lord' or 'having as a lord').

[337] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.98)
SANSKRIT: अधिः करोतौ विभाषा कर्मप्रवचनीयसंज्ञौ भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _adhi_ is optionally _karma_ _pravachaniya_ when the verb _kri_ follows.

[338] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.99)
SANSKRIT: लादेशाः परस्मैपदसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The substitutes of ल are called _parasmaipada_.

[339] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.100)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वेण परस्मै पदसंज्ञायां प्राप्तायां तङानयेरात्मनेपदसंज्ञा विधीयते ॥
ENGLISH: The nine affixes comprised under the _Pratyahara_ _tan_ and the two ending in _ana_ (_Sanach and _Kanach_, which are substitutes of ल are called _Atmanepada_.

[340] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.101)
SANSKRIT: तिङोऽष्टादश प्रत्ययाः नव परस्मैपद संज्ञकाः नवात्मनेपदसंज्ञकाः तत्रपरस्मैपदेषु त्रयस्त्रिकाः यथाक्रमम् प्रथममध्यमोत्तमसंज्ञा भवन्ति । आत्मनेपदेष्वपि त्रयास्त्रिकाः प्रथममध्यमोत्तमसंज्ञा भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The three triads in both the sets _Parasmaipada_ and _Atmanepada_, of conjugational affixes (comprised under the general name _tin_, a _pratyahara_ formed of the first and last of them, viz., _tip_ and _mahin_) are called, in order, Lowest (3rd person of European Grammar,) the middle (2nd person), and the highest (1st person).

[341] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.102)
SANSKRIT: तान्येकवचन द्विवचन बहुवचन संज्ञानि भवन्ति एकशः एकैकं पदम् ॥
ENGLISH: These three triads of conjugational affixes, which have received the names of Lowest &c, are called (as regard the three expressions in each triad) severally 'the expression for one' (singular) 'the expression for two' (dual), and 'the expression for many' (plural).

[342] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.103)
SANSKRIT: सुपञ्च त्रीणि त्रीणि पदानि एकश एकवचन द्विवचन बहुवचन संज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: Of _sup_ (which is a _Pratyahara_ formed of _su_ the first of the case affixes and the final _p_ of the last of them) the three expressions in each successive set of the three, are also severally called singular, dual and plural.

[343] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.104)
SANSKRIT: त्रीणि त्रीणि विभक्ति संज्ञाश्च भवन्ति सुपश्तिङञ्च ॥
ENGLISH: The traids of conjugational affixes and case affixes are also called _vibhakti_ or Inflective affixes.

[344] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.105)
SANSKRIT: युष्मद्युपपदे सति व्यवहिते चाव्यवहिते सति समानाधिकरणे समानाभिधेये तुल्यकारके स्थानिनि प्रयुज्यमानेऽप्यप्रयुज्यमानेऽपि मध्यम पुरुषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the pronoun _yushmad_, 'thou' understood, and also when the same expressed, is the attendant word in agreement with the verb, then there is the verbal termination called the middle (2nd person).

[345] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.106)
SANSKRIT: प्रहासे गम्यमाने मन्योपपदे धातोर्मध्यमपुरुषो भवति, मन्यतेश्चोत्तमः स चैकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When joke is implied with reference to an action, the verb denoting it is used in the 2nd person; provided that the word _manya_ 'to think' is the attendant word (_upapada_) of such verb, and of the verb _manya_ itself, the affix must be of the 1st person and singular number.

[346] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.107)
SANSKRIT: अस्मद्युपपदे समानाभिधेये प्रयुज्यमानेप्यप्रयुज्यमानेऽपि उत्तमपुरुषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the pronoun asmad 'I,' understood and also when expressed, is the attendant word in agreement with the verb, then there is the verbal termination called the Highest or the 1st person.

[347] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.108)
SANSKRIT: यत्र युष्मदस्मदी समानाधिकरणे उपपदेन स्तः तत्रशेषे प्रथमपुरुषो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the other cases, namely where, 'thou' or 'I' are not the attendant words in agreement with the verb, there is the verbal termination called the Lowest (or 3rd person).

[348] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.109)
SANSKRIT: परो यः संनिकर्षो वर्णानामर्द्धमात्राकालव्यवधानं स संहितासंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The closest proximity of letters, there being the intervention of half a _matra_ or prosodial length between them, is called contact or _sanhita_.

[349] panini_sutras.jsonl (1.4.110)
SANSKRIT: विरतिर्विरामः । विरम्यतेऽनेनेति वा विरामः । सोऽवसानसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The cessation or the absence of succeeding letters is called pause or _avasana_.

[350] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.1)
SANSKRIT: परिभाषेयम्, यः कश्चिदिह शास्त्रे पदविधिः श्रूयते स समर्थो वेदितव्यः॥
ENGLISH: A rule which relates to complete words (and not to the roots and affixes out of which the words are constructed) is to be understood to apply only to those words the senses of which are connected.

[351] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.2)
SANSKRIT: सुबन्तमामन्त्रिते परतः परस्याङ्गपद भवति , स्वरे, स्वरलक्षणे कर्तव्ये॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ सुबन्तस्य पराङ्गवद्भावे समानाधिकरणस्योपसंख्यानमननन्तरत्वात् ॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ अव्ययानां प्रतिषेधो वक्तव्यः ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending in sup or a case affix, when followed by a word in the vocative case, is regarded as if it was the _anga_ or component part of such subsequent vocative word, when a rule relating to accent is to be applied.

[352] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.3)
SANSKRIT: कडारसंशब्दात् प्राग् यानित ऊर्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामस्ते समाससंज्ञा वेदितव्याः॥
ENGLISH: From this point up to the aphorism _kadarakarmadháraye_ (II.2.38), all the terms that we shall say describe will get the designation of _samasa_ or compound.

[353] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.4)
SANSKRIT: सुबिति सहेति सुपेति च त्रयमप्यधिकृतं वेदितव्यम्। यदित ऊर्ध्वम् अनुक्रमिष्यामस्तत्रेदमुपस्थितं द्रष्टव्यम्॥
ENGLISH: The words '_saha supá_' meaning 'with a word ending in a case-affix,' are to be understood in each of the succeeding aphorisms.

[354] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.5)
SANSKRIT: यानित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामोऽव्ययीभावसंज्ञा स्ते वेदितव्याः॥
ENGLISH: From this forward whatever we shall describe will get the name of _Avyayibháva samasa_ or adverbial compound.

[355] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.6)
SANSKRIT: विभक्त्यादिष्वर्थेषु यदव्ययं वर्तते तत् समर्थेन सुबन्तेन सह समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: An indeclinable (_avaya_) employed with the sense of an inflective-affix (_vibhakti_) or of near to, or prosperity, or adversity, or absence of the thing, or departure, or not now, or the production of some sound, or after, or according to, or order of arrangement, or simultaneousness, or likeness, or possession, or totality, or termination, is invariably compounded with a word ending in a case-affix which is connected with it in sense, and the compound so formed is called _Avyayibhava_.

[356] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.7)
SANSKRIT: यथेत्येदव्ययमसादृश्ये वर्तमानां सुपा सह समस्यते, अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _yathá_ when it does not signify 'likeness' is invariably compounded with a word ending in a case-affix which is in construction with it and the compound is called an _Avyayibháva _Samása_.

[357] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.8)
SANSKRIT: यावदित्येतदव्ययमवधारणे वर्तमानं सुपा सह समस्यते, अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _yárat_ when it signifies limitation, is invariably compounded with a word ending in a case-affix which is in construction with it, and the compound so formed is called _Avyayibhåva_.

[358] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.9)
SANSKRIT: नानार्थे वर्तमानेन प्रतिना सह सुबन्तं समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: A word ending in a case-affix is compounded with the indeclinable word _prati_, when meaning a little and the compound is called _Avyayibháva_.

[359] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.10)
SANSKRIT: अक्षशब्दः शलाकाशब्दः संख्याशब्दश्च परिणा सह समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The words _aksha_, 'a die for playing with,' saláká ‘an ivory piece used in gambling,' and _sankhyá_ numerals' are compounded with the word _pari_, and the compound formed is called _Avyayibháva_.

[360] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.11)
SANSKRIT: यदित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामस्तद्विभाषा भवति॥
ENGLISH: The word _vibháshá_ or option governs all succeeding _sútras_.

[361] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.12)
SANSKRIT: अप परि बहिस् अञ्चु इत्येते सुबन्ताः पञ्चम्यन्तेन सह विभाषा समस्यन्ते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: ॥ The words _apa_, _pari_, _bahih_ and indeclinable ending in _añchu_ may optionally be compounded with a word ending in the fifth case-affix, and the compound so formed will be _Avyayibháva_.

[362] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.13)
SANSKRIT: आङित्येतन्मर्य्यादायामभिविधौ च वर्तमानं पञ्चम्यन्तेन सह विभाषा समस्यते, अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _áñ_ when signifying limit exclusive or limit inclusive, may optionally be compounded with a word ending in the fifth case-affix and the com. pound so formed is called _Avyayibháva_.

[363] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.14)
SANSKRIT: लक्षणं चिह्नं तद्वाचिना सुबन्तेन सहाभिप्रती-शब्दावाभिमुख्ये वर्तमानौ विभाषा समस्येते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The words _abhi_ and _prati_ when implying direction (towards), are optionally compounded with a word ending in a case-affix denoting the limit, or the goal which regulates or marks the direction; and the resulting compound is _Avyayibháva_ .

[364] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.15)
SANSKRIT: अनुर्यस्य समीपवाची तेन लक्षणाभूतेन सह विभाषा समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: The word _anu_ \'near to\' is optionally compounded with that word, nearness to which is indicated by the particle, and the resulting compound is _Avyayîbháva_.

[365] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.16)
SANSKRIT: अनुर्यस्यायामवाची तेन लक्षणभूतेन सह विभाषा समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The word anu 'alongside of' is optionally compounded with the word indicative of that whose length the particle expresses, and the compound so formed is called _Avyayibhåva_.

[366] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.17)
SANSKRIT: तिष्टद्ग्वादयः समुदाया एव निपात्यन्ते, तिष्टद्गुप्रभृतीनि शब्दरूपाणि अव्ययीभावसंज्ञानि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: And the words _tishthadgu_, 'at the time when the cows stand to be milked,' &c., are _Avyayibháva_ compounds.

[367] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.18)
SANSKRIT: पारमध्यशब्दौ षष्ठ्यन्तेन सह विभाषा समस्यते अव्ययीभाव समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The words _para_ across,' and _madhya_ middle' may optionally be compounded with a word ending in the sixth case-affix, when they take the forms _pare_ and _madhye_, and the compound so formed is _Avyayibhava_.

[368] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.19)
SANSKRIT: वंशे भवः वंश्यः, तद्वाचिना सुबन्तेन सह संख्या समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: A numeral may be compounded with a word denoting 'one belonging to a family' and the resulting compound is _Avyayibhiva_.

[369] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.20)
SANSKRIT: नदीवचनैः शब्दैः सह संख्या समस्यते, अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A numeral (_sankhyâ_) may be compounded with names of 'rivers’ and the resulting compound is _Avyayibhava_ denoting an aggregate.

[370] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.21)
SANSKRIT: नदीभिः सह सुबन्तमन्यपदार्थे वर्तमानं संज्ञायां विषये समस्यते अव्ययीभावश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending in a case-affix is compounded with words denoting the names of rivers, when the compouud word denotes a thing other than that expressed by the terms of the compound, and is an appellative ; the compound so formed being an _Avyayibhåva_.

[371] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.22)
SANSKRIT: तत्पुरुष इति संज्ञाधिक्रियते प्राग्बहुब्रीहे, र्यानित ऊर्द्धवमनुक्रमिष्यामस्तत्पुरुषसंज्ञास्ते वेदितव्याः ॥
ENGLISH: From this aphorism as far as aphorism II. 2. 23, the word _Tat-purusha_ is the governing word, and is understood in all the following _sůtras_.

[372] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.23)
SANSKRIT: द्विगुश्च समासस्तत्पुरुषसंज्ञो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the kind of compound called _Dwigu_ (Numeral Determinative Compound) is also called _Tat-purusha_.

[373] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.24)
SANSKRIT: द्वितीयान्तं सुबन्तं श्रितादिभिः सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the second case-affix is compounded with the words _srita_ 'who has had recourse to,'_atîta_, 'gone by, '_patita_ 'who has fallen upon, '_gata_, 'who has gone to, '_atyasta_, 'who has passed, '_prapta_, 'who has obtained, 'and _åpanna_, 'who has reached, 'and the resulting compound is called _Tat-purusha_.

[374] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.25)
SANSKRIT: स्वयमित्येतत्सुबन्तं क्तान्तेन सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _swayam_ 'oneself' is compounded with a word ending in the affix _kta_, and the resulting compound is called _Tat-purusha_.

[375] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.26)
SANSKRIT: खट्वाशब्दो द्वितीयान्तः क्तान्तेन सह क्षेपे गम्यमाने समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _khatwå_ in the accusative case is compounded with a word ending in the affix _kta_, when censure is implied, and the compound so formed is _Tat purusha_.

[376] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.27)
SANSKRIT: सामीत्येतदव्ययमर्द्धशब्दपर्यायस्तस्य सन्त्ववाचित्वाद् द्वितीयया नास्ति संबन्धः तत् सुबन्तं क्तान्तेन सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _såmi_ meaning 'half' is compounded with a word ending in the affix _kta_, and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[377] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.28)
SANSKRIT: कालवाचिनः शब्दा द्वितीयान्ताः क्तान्तेन सह समस्यन्ते विभाषा तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words denoting time, being in the accusative case, are optionally compounded with a word ending in the affix _kta_, and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[378] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.29)
SANSKRIT: कालवाचिनः शब्दा द्वितीयान्ता अत्यन्तसंयोगे गम्यमाने सुपा सह समस्यन्ते विभाषा तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Words denoting time being in the accusative case, are optionally compounded with a word ending in a case-affix, when used in the sense of complete connection throughout with the time (i.e., duration of time) and the resulting compound is called _Tat-purusha_

[379] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.30)
SANSKRIT: तृतीयान्तं सुबन्तं गुणवचनेन अर्थशब्देन च सह समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 3rd case-affix is optionally compounded with what denotes quality, the quality being that which is instrumentally caused by the thing signified by what ends with the third case affix and with the word _artha_ 'wealth,' and the compound so formned is called _Tat-purusha_.

[380] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.31)
SANSKRIT: पूर्व सदृश सम ऊनार्थ कलह निपुण मिश्र श्लक्षण एतैः सह तृतीयान्तं समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति॥ वार्त्तिकम् ॥ पूर्वादिष्ववरस्योपसंख्यानम् ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending in the third case-affix is compounded with the words _purva_, '_prior_,' _sadrisa_ 'like,' _sama_ 'similar, '_ûnartha_, 'words having the sense of less, '_kalaha_ 'quarrel,'_nipuna_, 'proficient,' _misra_ 'mixed,' and _slakshņa_ 'polished sleek' and the resulting compound is called _Tat-purusha_.

[381] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.32)
SANSKRIT: कर्तरि करणे च या तृतीया तदन्तं कृदन्तेन सह समस्यते बहुलं, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the third case-affix, when it denotes the agent or the instrument (II. 3. 18) is compounded diversely with what ends with a _krit_ affix; and the compound so formed is called _Tat-purusha_.

[382] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.33)
SANSKRIT: कर्तृ करण्योर्या तृतीया तदन्तं सुबन्तं कृत्यैः सह समस्यते अधिकार्थचने गम्यमाने विभाषा, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the third case-affix when it denotes the agent or the instrument, is compounded optionally with a word ending in a _kritya_ affix, when an exaggerated statement (whether of praise or of censure) is implied, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[383] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.34)
SANSKRIT: व्यंजनवाचि तृतीयान्तमन्नवाचिना सुबन्तेन सह समस्यते विभाषा, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the third case-affix and denoting a condiment, is optionally compounded with a word ending in a case-affix, signifying food, and the resulting compound is called _Tat-purusha_.

[384] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.35)
SANSKRIT: मिश्रीकरणवाचि तृतीयान्तं भक्ष्यवाचिना सुबन्तेन सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word, ending with the 3rd case-affix and denoting a relish giving or refining ingredient, is compounded with a word meaning, victuals, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[385] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.36)
SANSKRIT: तदर्थ अर्थ बलि हित सुख रक्षित इत्येतैः सह चतुर्थ्यन्तं समस्यते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ।। वार्तिकम् ।। अर्थेन नित्यसमासवचनं सर्व लिङ्गता च वक्तव्या
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 4th case-affix is compounded with what denotes that which is for the purpose of what ends with the 4th case-affix and so too with the words _artha_ 'on account of,' _bali_ 'a sacrifice,' _hita_ 'salutary,' _sukha_ 'pleasure' and _rakshita_ 'kept,' and the compound is called _Tat-purusa_.

[386] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.37)
SANSKRIT: पञ्चम्यन्तं सुबन्तं भयशब्देन सुबन्तेन सह समस्यते विभाषा तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ भय भीत भीति भीभिरिति वक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 5th case-affix is optionally compounded with the word _bhaya_ 'fear,' and the compound is _Tat-purusa_.

[387] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.38)
SANSKRIT: अपेत अपौढ़ मुक्त पतित अपत्रस्त इत्येतैः सह पञ्चम्यन्तं समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥ अल्पश इति समासस्याल्पविषयतामाचष्टे ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 5th case-affix is compounded with the words _apeta_ 'gone away,' _apodha_ 'carried away,' _mukta_ 'freed,' _patita_ 'fallen,' _apatrasta_, 'afraid of,' when the event takes place in a gradual manner, and the compound is called _Tat-purusa_.

[388] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.39)
SANSKRIT: स्तोक अन्तिक दूर इत्येवमर्थाः शब्दा कृच्छ्र शदश्च पञ्चम्यन्ताः क्तान्तेन सह समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Words with the sense of _stoka_ 'a little,' _antika_ 'near,' _dúra_ 'far,' and also the word _krichchhra_ ‘penance,' ending in the 5th case-affix are compounded with what ends in _kta_, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[389] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.40)
SANSKRIT: सप्तम्यन्तं शौण्डादिभिः सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 7th case-affix is compounded with the words _šaunda_ ‘skilled' &c. and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[390] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.41)
SANSKRIT: सिद्ध-शुष्क-पक्व-बन्धै इत्येतैः सह सप्तम्यन्तं समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 7th case-affix is compounded with the words _siddha_ ‘perfected,' _šushka_ ‘dried,' _pakva_ 'cooked' and _bandha_ 'bound,' and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[391] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.42)
SANSKRIT: ध्वाङ्क्षवाचिना सह सप्तम्यन्तं सुबन्तं समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति, क्षेपे गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 7th case-affix is compounded with the word _dhvánksha_, 'a crow,' (and with synonyms of crow) when contempt is implied; and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[392] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.43)
SANSKRIT: कृत्य प्रत्ययान्तैः सह सप्तम्यन्तं समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 7th case-affix is compounded with words ending with a _kritya_-affix (a fut. pass. Participle) and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_, when 'debt' is implied.

[393] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.44)
SANSKRIT: संज्ञायां विषये सप्तम्यन्तं सुपा सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the 7th case-affix is invariably compounded with a word ending in a case-affix, when the compound thus formed is used as an appellative, and is called _Tat-purusha_ _samása_.

[394] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.45)
SANSKRIT: अहरवयवा रात्र्यवयवाश्च सप्तम्यन्ताः क्तान्तेन सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The names of divisions of day or night ending with a 7th case-affix are compounded with words ending with the affix _kta_; and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[395] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.46)
SANSKRIT: तत्रेत्येतत् सप्तम्यन्तं क्तान्तेन सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _tatra_ ‘there,' which is a word ending with the 7th case-affix, (V. 3. 10) is compounded with a word ending in _kta_, and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[396] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.47)
SANSKRIT: क्षेपे गम्यमाने सप्तम्यन्तं क्तान्तेन सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a 7th case-affix is compounded with a word ending with the affix _kta_, when 'censure' is implied, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[397] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.49)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वकाल एक सर्व जरत् पुराण नव केवल इत्येते सुबन्ताः समानाधिकरणेन सुपा सह समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting an action which naturally precedes in time (_púrvakála_), and the words _eka_' one,' _sarva_ 'all,' _jarat_ 'old,' _purána_ 'ancient,’ _nava- 'new' and _kevala_ 'only,' are compounded with their co-relative case-inflected words which are in the same case with them; and the compound so formed is _Tat-purusha_.

[398] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.50)
SANSKRIT: दिग्वाचिनः शब्दाः संख्या च समानाधिकरणेन सुबन्तेन सह समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: Tho words expressing a point of the compass, or a number (_sankhyá_) enter into composition with the word correlated to them by being in the same case, when the sense of the compound is that of an appellative; and it is a _Tat-purusha_.

[399] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.51)
SANSKRIT: तद्धितार्थे विषये उत्तरपदे च परतः समाहारे चाभिधेये दिसंख्ये समानाधिकरणेन सुपा सह समस्येते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: In a case where the sense is that of a _Taddhita_ -aflix, or when an additional member comes after the compound, or when an aggregate. is to be expressed, then a word signifying a point of the compass or a number, enters into composition with a case-inflected word which is in agreement with it by being in the same case, and the compound so formed is called _Tat-purusha_.

[400] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.52)
SANSKRIT: तद्धितार्थोत्तरपदसमाहारे चेत्यत्र यः संख्यापूर्वः समासः द्विगुसंज्ञो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: In a case where the sense is that of a _taddhita_ affix, or when an additional member comes after the compound or when an aggregate is to be expressed. the compound, the first member of which is numeral, is called _Dvigu_ or Numeral Determinative compound.

[401] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.53)
SANSKRIT: कुत्सितवाचीनि सुबन्तानि कुत्सनवचनैः सुबन्तैः सह समस्यन्ते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति।।
ENGLISH: Case-inflected words expressing vileness are compounded with case-inflected words, expressing contempt, and the resulting compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[402] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.54)
SANSKRIT: पाप अणक एते सुबन्ते कुत्सितवचनैः सह समस्येते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति।।
ENGLISH: The case inflected words _pápa_ 'sin,' and _anaka_ 'insignificant,' are compounded with words expressive of vileness, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[403] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.55)
SANSKRIT: उपमानवाचीनि सुबन्तानि सामान्यवचनैः सुबन्तैः सह समस्यन्ते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति।।
ENGLISH: Case-inflected words denoting objects of comparison, are compounded with words denoting what is likened to them, by reason of the latter possessing qualities in common with the former, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[404] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.56)
SANSKRIT: उपमेयमुपमितं तद्वाचि सुबन्तं व्याघ्रादिभिः सामर्थ्याद् उपमानवचनैः सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति , न चेत्सामान्यवाचि शब्दः प्रयुज्यते ।।
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting subject of comparison is compounded with the words _vyåghra_ 'tigur,' &c., the latter being the standard of comparison, and in construction with the former; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_; provided that any word expressing the common characteristic (सामान्य) as explained above, is not employed.

[405] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.57)
SANSKRIT: विशेषणवाचि सुबन्तं विशेष्यवाचिना समानाधिकरणेन सुबन्तेन सह बहुलं समस्यते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting the qualifier (the Adjective), is compounded diversely with a case inflected word denoting the thing thereby qualified, (the Substantive) the latter being in agreement (same case) with the former; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[406] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.58)
SANSKRIT: पूर्व अपर प्रथम चरम जघन्य समान मध्य मध्यम वीर इत्येते सुबन्ताः समानाधिकरणेन सुपा सह समस्यन्ते , तत्परुषश्च समासो भवति ।।
ENGLISH: The case-inflected words _purva_ 'prior,' _apara_ 'other,' _prathama_ 'first,' _charama_ 'last,' _jaghanya_ 'hindmost,' _samâna_ 'equal,' _madhya_ 'middle,' _madhyama_ 'middle,' and _vira_. 'hero,' are compounded with words ending with a case-affix and which are in agreement (same case) with them; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[407] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.59)
SANSKRIT: श्रेण्यादयः सुबन्ता कृतादिभिः समानाधिकरणैः समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ श्रेण्यादिषु च्व्यर्थवचनम् ॥
ENGLISH: The case-inflected words _śreni_ 'class' &c., are compounded with words _krita_ 'made,'&c., which are in agreement (same case), with them; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[408] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.60)
SANSKRIT: नञैव विशेषो यस्य सर्वमन्यत् प्रकृत्यादिकं तुल्यं तन्नञ्विशिष्टं तेन नञ्विशिष्टेन क्तान्तेन समानाधिकरणेन सह अनञ् क्तान्तं समस्यते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ कृतापकृतादीनामुपसंख्यानम् ॥ वार्तिकम् ॥ समानाधिकरणाधिकारे शाकपार्थिवादीनामुपसंख्यानम् , उत्तरपदलोपश्च ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the affix _kta_, and not having the negative augment nan is compounded with the same word ending with the affix _kta_ but which is distinguished from the former, by having the augment nan; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[409] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.61)
SANSKRIT: सत् महत् परम उत्तम उत्कृष्ट इत्येते पूज्यमानैः सह समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _sat_ ‘good,' _mahat_ 'great,' _parama_ ‘highest,' _uttama_ 'best,' and _utkrishta_ ‘excellent,' are compounded with the words denoting the person deserving of respect; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[410] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.62)
SANSKRIT: वृन्दारक नाग कुञ्जर इत्येतैः सह पूज्यमानवाचि सुबन्तं समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting object deserving of respect is compounded with the words _vrindáraka_ 'eminent,' _nága_ 'serpent or elephant,' _kuħjara_ 'elephant'; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[411] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.63)
SANSKRIT: कतर-कतमौ जाति-परिप्रश्ने वर्तमानौ समर्थेन सुपा सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _katara_ ‘which or who of two’ and _katama_ 'which or who of many', when used in asking questions about the genus or class, are compounded with other case-inflected words with which they are in construction, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[412] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.64)
SANSKRIT: किमित्येतत् क्षेपे गम्यमाने सुपा सह समस्यते , तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _kim_ 'what,' when implying 'contempt,’ is compounded with a word ending in a case-affix, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[413] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.65)
SANSKRIT: पोटादिभिः सह जातिवाचि सुबन्तं समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting a genus (_játi_) is compounded with the words _potá_ ‘a hermaphrodite,' _yuvati_, 'a young female,' _stoka_ 'a little,' _katipaya_ 'a few,' _grishţi_ 'a cow which has had only one calf,' _dhenu_ 'milch-cow,' _vaśá_, 'a barren female,' _vehad_, 'a cow that miscarries,' _bashkayani_ 'a cow that has a full-grown calf,' _pravaktri_ 'an expounder,' _śrotriya_ 'a learned _Brahmana_,’ _adhyapaka_, 'a teacher,' and _dhúrta_ ‘a cunning fellow; and the resulting compound is called -Tat-purusha_.

[414] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.66)
SANSKRIT: जातिवाचि सुबन्तं प्रशंसावचनैः सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting a genus (_játi_) is compounded with a word denoting praise and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[415] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.67)
SANSKRIT: खलत्यादिभिः समानाधिकरणैः सह युवशब्दः समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _yuvá_ 'young,' is compounded with the words _khalati_, 'bald headed,' _palita_ 'grey-haired' _valina_ 'wrinkled,' _jarati_ 'decayed,' when they are in agreement (same case); and the compound is _Tat purusha_.

[416] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.68)
SANSKRIT: कृत्य-प्रत्ययान्तास्तुल्यपर्यायाश्च सुबन्ता अजातिवचनेन समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Words ending with a _kritya_ affix, and the word _tulya_ 'equal,' and its synonyms, are compounded with words which do not denote genus (_játi_) being in the same case with them; and the compound is _Tatpurusha_.

[417] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.69)
SANSKRIT: वर्णविशेषवाचि सुबन्त वर्णविशेषवाचिना सुबन्तेन समानाधिकरणेन सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting 'colour' is compounded with another case-inflected word which is in agreement with the former, and also denotes colour, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[418] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.70)
SANSKRIT: कुमारशब्दः श्रमणादिभिः सह समस्यते, तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _kumára_ 'a boy' is compounded with co-ordinate words _śramaná_ 'an ascetic,' &c., and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[419] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.71)
SANSKRIT: चतुष्पादवाचिनः सुबन्ता गर्भिणी शब्देन समस्यन्ते, तत्पुरुषश समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A case-inflected word denoting a quadruped is compounded with the co-ordinate word _garbhiní_, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[420] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.1.72)
SANSKRIT: समुदाया एव निपात्यन्ते मयूरव्यंसकादयः शब्दास्तत्पुरुषसंज्ञाश्च भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And the words _mayúra-vyamsaká_ 'cunning like a peacock,' &c., are _Tat-purusha_ compounds.

[421] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.1)
SANSKRIT: एकदेशोऽस्यास्तीत्येकदेशी अवयवी तद्वाचिना सुबन्तेन सह पूर्वापराधरोत्तरशब्दाः सामर्थ्यादेकदेशवचनाः समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: The words _purva_ 'front', _apara_ 'near', _adhara_ 'lower', and _uttara_ 'upper', are compounded when in construction with a word signifying a thing that has parts, provided that the thing having parts is distinguished numerically by unity; and the compound is Tat-purusha.

[422] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.2)
SANSKRIT: अर्द्धमित्येतन्नपुंसकमेकदेशिनैकाधिकरणेन समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word अर्द्ध _ardha_ when it signifles exactly equal parts i. e. halves, is always neuter, and is compounded with a word signifying a thing that has parts, provided that the thing halved is numerically one; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[423] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.3)
SANSKRIT: द्वितीयादीनि शब्दरूपाणि एकदेशिनैकाधिकरणेन सहान्यतरस्यां समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The words _dvitiya_, 'second,' _tritiya_ 'third,' _chaturtha_ 'fourth,' and _turya_ 'fourth,' are optionally compounded with that word which signifies a thing that has parts, provided that the thing having parts is distinguished numerically by unity. The compound so formed is called _Tat-purusha_.

[424] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.4)
SANSKRIT: प्राप्त आपन्न इत्येतौ द्वितीयान्तेन सह समस्येते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति
ENGLISH: The words _prapta_ 'obtained,' and _apanna_ 'obtained' are optionally compounded with words ending in a second case-affix and form _Tat-purusha_ compound.

[425] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.5)
SANSKRIT: परिमाणमस्यास्तीति परिमाणी तद्वाचिना सुबन्तेन सह सामर्थ्यात् परिमाणवचनाः कालशब्दाः समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति
ENGLISH: Words denoting time are compounded when in construction, with words denoting the object whose duration is measured by the time, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[426] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.6)
SANSKRIT: नञ् समर्थन सुबन्तेन सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥ वार्त्तिकम् ॥ नञो नलोपस्तिङि क्षेपे ॥
ENGLISH: The negative word _nañ_ is compounded with a case-inflected word with which it is in construction, and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[427] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.7)
SANSKRIT: ईषदित्ययं शब्दोऽकृदन्तेन सुपा सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति॥ वार्त्तिकम् ॥ ईषद् गुणवचनेनेतिवक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: The word _Ishat_ 'a little,' is compounded with a case-inflected word which does not end with a _krit_-affix; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_

[428] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.8)
SANSKRIT: षष्ठ्यन्तं सुबन्तं समर्थेन सुभन्तेन सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति । वार्त्तिकम् ॥ कृद्योगा च षष्ठी समस्यत इति वक्तव्यम्॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is compounded with a case-inflected word with which it is in construction; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[429] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.9)
SANSKRIT: याजकादिभिः सह षष्ठी समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति । वार्त्तिकम् ॥ तस्स्थैश्च गुणैः षष्ठी समस्यत इति वक्तव्यम्॥वार्त्तिकम् गुणोत्तरेण तरलोपश्चेति वक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is compounded with the word yâjaka 'sacrificer' &c., and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[430] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.11)
SANSKRIT: पूरण गुणसुहितार्थ सद् अव्यय तव्य समानाधिकरण इत्येतैः सह षष्ठी न समस्यते ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is not compounded with a word having the sense of an ordinal, an attribute, or satisfaction, or with a participle ending in the affix called '_sat_' (III. 2. 127) or an indeclinable, or ending with the atfix _tarya_, or with a word denoting the same object (i. e., when they are in apposition).

[431] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.12)
SANSKRIT: को वः पूजायां विहितस्तेन पष्ठी न समस्यते ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is not compounded with a word ending with the affix '_kta_,' when the force of '_kta_' is to denote 'respect' &c.

[432] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.13)
SANSKRIT: अधिकरणवाचिना क्तेन षष्ठी न समस्यते ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is not compounded with a word ending with the affix '_kta_,' when the force of the latter is to denote 'locality' in which something has happened.

[433] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.14)
SANSKRIT: कर्मणि च या षष्ठी सा न समस्यते ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is not compounded with another, when the force of the genitive case is that of the accusative.

[434] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.16)
SANSKRIT: कर्तरि च यौ तृजकौ ताभ्यां सह षष्ठी न समस्यते ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with the sixth case-affix is not compounded with a word ending with '_trich_'or '_aka_' affix when the force of these latter affixes is that of an agent.

[435] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.17)
SANSKRIT: क्रीडायां जीविकायां च नित्यं पष्ठी समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word ending with a sixth case-affix is invariably compounded with a word ending with _aka_, when these affixes denote a sport or a livelihood; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[436] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.18)
SANSKRIT: कु गति प्रादयः समर्थेन शब्दान्तरेन सह नित्यं समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुष समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The indeclinable word _ku_ 'bad,' the particles called _gati_, and the prepositions _pra_, &c., are invariably compounded with other words with which they are in construction; and the resulting compound is _Tat purusha_.

[437] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.19)
SANSKRIT: उपपदमतिङन्तं समर्थेन शब्दान्तरेण सह समस्यते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An upapada or attendant word (III. 1. 92), which does not end with a tense-affix (III. 4. 78) is in variably compounded with that with which it is in construction. The compound thus formed is _Tat-purusha_.

[438] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.20)
SANSKRIT: अव्ययेनोपपदस्य यः समासः सोऽमैव भवति नान्येन ॥
ENGLISH: When an _upapada_ is compounded with an indeclinable , then it is compounded only with those _avyayas_ which end in the affix अम्.

[439] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.21)
SANSKRIT: उपदंशस्तृतीयायामित्यतः प्रभृति यान्युपपदानि तान्यमैवाव्ययेन सहान्यतरस्यां समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An _upapada_ ending with a third case-affix (III. 4. 47) &c., is compounded optionally with an indeclinable formed by the affix अम् and the compound is _Tatpurusha_.

[440] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.22)
SANSKRIT: क्त्वाप्रत्ययेन सह तृतोया प्रभॄतीन्युपपदानि अन्यतरस्यां समस्यन्ते तत्पुरुषश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An _upapada_ ending with a third case-affix or any other of the remaining four cases, is optionally compounded with a word ending with the affix _ktva_; and the compound is _Tat-purusha_.

[441] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.23)
SANSKRIT: शेषः समासो बहुव्रीहिसंज्ञी भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The remaining compound is called _Bahuvrihi_.

[442] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.24)
SANSKRIT: अनेकं सुबन्तमन्यपदार्थे वर्त्तमानं समस्यते बहुव्रीहिश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Two or more words, ending in any case affix, form a compound, denoting another new thing, not connoted by those words individually; and the compound is called Bahuvrihi.

[443] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.25)
SANSKRIT: संख्येये या संख्या वर्त्तते तया सहाव्ययासन्नादूराधिकसंख्याः समस्यन्ते बहुव्रीहिश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: Indeclinable words and the words _asanna_ near,' _adūra_ 'near,' _adhika_ 'more' and the words called _sankhyå_ (Numerals) are compounded, with another _sankhyå_ word, when the sense is that of a numeral or _sankhyå_. The compound is _Bahuvrihi_.

[444] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.26)
SANSKRIT: दिङ्नामानि सुबन्तानि अन्तराले वाच्ये समस्यन्ते बहुव्रीहिश्च समासो भवति॥
ENGLISH: Words which are the names of the points of the compass are compounded, when the compound signifies the intermediate point, and the compound so formed is _Bahuvrihi_.

[445] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.27)
SANSKRIT: तत्रेति सतम्यन्ते सरूपे परे तेनेति च तृतीयान्ते इदमित्येतस्मिन्नर्थे समस्यते बहुव्रीहिश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Two homonymous words both being in the locative case or both being in the instrumental case) are compounded, the sense being 'this happens therein or with that.' The compound so formed is Bahuvrihi.

[446] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.28)
SANSKRIT: सहेत्येतच्छत्रुरूप तुल्ययोगे वर्नमानं तेनेति तृतीयान्तेन सह समस्यते बहुव्रीहिश्च समासो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _saha_ 'together is compounded with a word ending with the third case-affix and the compound is _Bahuvrihi_; provided that, the companion and the person accompanied are equally affected by any, action or thing, in the same manner.

[447] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.29)
SANSKRIT: अनेकं सुबन्तं चार्थे वर्त्तमानं समस्यते द्वन्द्वश्च समासो भवति ।
ENGLISH: When a set of several words ending with case-affixes stands in a relation expressible by 'and' the set is made into a compound; and the compound so formed is called _Dvandva_.

[448] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.30)
SANSKRIT: उपसर्जनसंशक समासे पूर्व प्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: The _upasarjana_ ( I. 2. 43 ) is to be placed first in a compound.

[449] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.31)
SANSKRIT: राजदन्तादिषु परमुपसर्जनं प्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: the _upasarjana_ is to be put last in the words _Rajadanta_ &c.

[450] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.32)
SANSKRIT: द्वन्द्वे समासे घ्यन्तं पूर्वं प्रयोक्तम्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: In a _Dvandva_ compound, let a word called घि (I. 4.7) stand first.

[451] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.33)
SANSKRIT: अजाद्यदन्तं शब्दरूपं द्वन्द्वे समासे पूर्वं प्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: 33. In a _Drandva_ compound, let what begins with a vowel and ends with a short अ be placed first.

[452] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.34)
SANSKRIT: अल्पाच्तरं शब्दरूपं द्वन्द्वे समासे पूर्वप्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: In a _Dvandva_ compound, that word-form which has fewer vowels, is to be placed first.

[453] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.35)
SANSKRIT: सप्तम्यन्तं विशेषणं च बहुव्रीहिसमासे पूर्वं प्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: A word with the seventh case-affix and an epithet are to be placed first in the _Bahuvrihi_ compound.

[454] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.36)
SANSKRIT: निष्टान्तं च बहुव्रीहिसमासे पूर्वं प्रयोक्तव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: What ends with a _Nishtha_(I.1.26.) shall stand first in a _Bahuvrihi_ compound.

[455] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.37)
SANSKRIT: आहिताग्न्यादिषु निष्ठान्तं पूर्वं वा प्रयोज्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: In the compounds _Ahitågni_ and the like, the _Nishtha_-formed word may optionally be placed first.

[456] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.2.38)
SANSKRIT: कडारादयः शब्दाः कर्मधारये समासे वा पूर्वं प्रयोक्तव्याः ॥
ENGLISH: The words _kadarah_ and the like, are optionally placed first in the _karma-dharaya_.

[457] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.1)
SANSKRIT: अनभिहित इत्यधिकारोऽयं वेदितव्यः । यदित उर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामोऽनभिहित इत्येवं तद्वेदितव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: The word '_anabhihite_' meaning 'not being specified' is to be understood as the governing word.

[458] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.2)
SANSKRIT: कर्मणि कारके या संख्या तत्र द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the object is not denoted by the termination of the verb, i e. when the verb does not agree with it, the second case-affix is attached to the word.

[459] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.3)
SANSKRIT: छन्दसि विषये जुहोतेः कर्मणि कारके तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति द्वितीया च ॥
ENGLISH: In the _chhandas_ (_veda_), the object of the verb _hu_ 'to sacrifice' takes the affix of the third case, and of the second as well.

[460] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.4)
SANSKRIT: अन्तराऽन्तरेणशब्दौ निपातौ साहचर्याद् गृह्येते ॥
ENGLISH: A word joined with (or governed by) the word _antara_, or _antarena_ takes the second case-affix.

[461] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.5)
SANSKRIT: काल शब्देभ्योऽध्वशब्देभ्यश्च द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति अत्यन्तसंयोगे गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: After a word denoting time, or length, the affix of the 2nd case is employed, when denoting full duration.

[462] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.6)
SANSKRIT: अपवर्गे गम्यमाने कालाध्वनोरत्यन्तसंयोगे तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The third case-affix is employed after the words denoting the duration of time or place, when the accomplishment of the desired object is meant to be expressed.

[463] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.7)
SANSKRIT: कारकयोर्मध्ये यौ कालाध्वानौ ताभ्यां सप्तमी पञ्चम्यौ विभक्ती भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: A noun denoting time or place gets the affix of the seventh or the fifth case, when the sense implied is that the time or space is the interval between one action and another action (or implies an interval of time and space between two _karakas_).

[464] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.8)
SANSKRIT: कर्मप्रवचनीयैर्युक्ते द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The second case-affix is employed after a word which is joined with a _karmapravachaniya_ (I. 4. 83).

[465] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.9)
SANSKRIT: यस्मादधिकं यस्य चेश्वरवचनं कर्मप्रवचनीयैर्युक्ते तत्र सप्तमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Where a word is governed by a _karmapravachaniya_ in the sense of 'more than' (I. 4. 87) or 'lord of' (I. 4. 97) there the 7th case-affix (locative) is employed.

[466] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.10)
SANSKRIT: अप आङ् परि इत्येतैः कर्मप्रवचनीयैर्योगे पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The fifth case-affix (Ablative) is employed when a word is governed by the following _karmapravachaniyas_ i.e., _apa_, _an_ and _pari_.

[467] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.11)
SANSKRIT: यस्मात्प्रतिनिधिर्यतश्च प्रतिदानं तत्र कर्मप्रवचनीययुक्ते पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The 5th case-affix (ablative) is employed after whatsoever is governed by a _karmapravachaniya_ in the sense of 'substitute' or 'exchange' (I. 4.92).

[468] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.12)
SANSKRIT: गत्यर्थानां धातूनां चेष्टाक्रियाणां परिस्पन्दक्रियाणां कर्मणि कारकेऽध्ववर्जिते द्वितीयाचतुर्थ्यौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: In the case of roots implying motion, the place to which motion is directed takes the affix of the 2nd (Accusative) or the 4th (Dative) case in denoting the 'object,' when physical motion is meant, and the object is not a word expressing 'road'.

[469] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.13)
SANSKRIT: संप्रदाने कारके चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In denoting the _sampradana_-_karaka_ (I. 4. 32.) the fourth affix or the Dative is employed after the noun.

[470] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.14)
SANSKRIT: क्रियार्थोपपदस्य च स्थानिनोऽप्रयुज्यमानस्य धातोः कर्मणि कारके चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The fourth case-affix is employed in denoting the object (_karma_) of that verb, which is suppressed (_sthani_) in a sentence, and which has in construction (_upapada_) therewith another verb, denoting an action, performed for the sake of the future action (_kriyarthe_ (III. 3. 10.)).

[471] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.15)
SANSKRIT: तुमर्थभाववचनप्रत्ययान्तात् प्रातिपदिकाच्चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The fourth case-affix is employed after a crude-form which ends in an affix denoting 'condition' (abstract noun, (III. 3. 11)) and having the force of the affix _tum_ (or Infinitive of purpose).

[472] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.16)
SANSKRIT: नमः स्वस्ति स्वाहा स्वधा अलं वषट् इत्येतयोर्योगे चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The fourth case-affix is employed in conjunction with the words _namah_ 'salutation,' _svasti_ 'peace,' _svaha_, _svadha_ (terms used in offering oblations to Gods and _Pitris_ respectively), _alam_ 'a match for' 'sufficient for' and _vashat_ a term of oblation.

[473] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.17)
SANSKRIT: मन्यकर्मणि प्राणिवर्जिते विभाषा चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति अनादरे गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: In denoting the indirect object, which is not an animal, of the verb _manya_ 'to think,' the dative case is optionally employed, when contempt is to be shown.

[474] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.18)
SANSKRIT: कर्तरि करणे च कारके तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In denoting the agent (I. 4. 54) or the instrument (I. 4. 42), the third case-affix is employed.

[475] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.19)
SANSKRIT: सहार्थेन युक्तेऽप्रधाने तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the word सह 'with,' is joined to a word the latter takes the third case, when the sense is that the word in the third case is not the principal but the accompaniment of the principal thing.

[476] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.20)
SANSKRIT: येनाङ्गेन विकृतेनाङ्गिनो विकारो लक्ष्यते ततस्तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: By whatsoever limb, being defective, is pointed out the defect of the person, after that the third case-affix is employed.

[477] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.21)
SANSKRIT: कंचित्प्रकारं प्राप्तः इत्थंभूतः । तस्य लक्षणमित्थं भूतलक्षणं, ततस्तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Any mark or attribute, by which is indicated the existence of a particular state or condition, is put in the third case to express this relation.

[478] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.22)
SANSKRIT: संपूर्वस्य जानातेः कर्मणि कारके द्वितीयायां प्राप्तायामन्यतरस्यां तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: After the verb _sam_-_jna_ the third case-affix is optionally employed in denoting the object.

[479] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.23)
SANSKRIT: फलसाधनयोग्यः पदार्थो लोके हेतुरुच्यते, तद्वाचिनस्तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When a word denotes 'cause,' it takes the third case-affix.

[480] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.24)
SANSKRIT: कर्तृवर्जितं यदृणं हेतुस्ततः पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A word, implying debt, considered as a 'cause' but not as a _kartri_ or agent, takes the fifth case-affix.

[481] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.25)
SANSKRIT: गुणे हेतावस्त्रीलिङ्गे विभाषा पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The fifth case-affix is employed optionally when the noun expresses an attribute, being the cause of an action, and not being of the feminine gender.

[482] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.26)
SANSKRIT: हेतुशब्दस्य प्रयोगे हेतौ द्योत्ये षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed after a noun implying the cause of an action, when the word _hetu_ is used along with such a word.

[483] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.27)
SANSKRIT: सर्वनाम्नो हेतुशब्दप्रयोगे हेतौ द्योत्ये तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति षष्ठी च ॥
ENGLISH: After a _sarvanaman_ (I. 1.27) when it signifies the cause of an action, and the word _hetu_ is used with it, the third case-affix is employed, as well as the sixth.

[484] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.28)
SANSKRIT: अपादाने कारके पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the _Apadana_-_karaka_ (I 4. 24) is denoted, the fifth case-affix is employed.

[485] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.29)
SANSKRIT: अन्य आरात् इतर ऋते दिक् शब्द अञ्चूत्तरपद आच् आहि इत्येतैर्योगे पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When a noun is joined with words meaning 'other than' or with _arat_ 'near or remote' or _itara_ 'different from' or _rite_ 'without,' or words indicative of the 'directions' (used also with reference to the time corresponding to them) or with words having _anchu_ 'to bend' as the last member of the compound (and expressive of direction), or with words ending with the affix _ach_ or _ahi_ (V. 3. 36) and (V. 3. 37) the fifth case-affix is employed.

[486] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.30)
SANSKRIT: अतसर्थेन प्रत्ययेन युक्ते षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed when used in connection with words ending with affixes having the sense of the affix _atasuch_ (V. 3. 28).

[487] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.31)
SANSKRIT: एनवन्वतरस्यामदूरे पञ्चम्या इति वक्ष्यति । तेन युक्ते द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: With a word ending with the affix '_enap_' (V. 3. 35), the second case-affix is employed as well as the sixth.

[488] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.32)
SANSKRIT: पृथक् विना नाना इत्येतैर्योगे तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति अन्यतरस्यां पञ्चमी च ॥
ENGLISH: When joined with the words _prithak_ 'without', _vina_ 'without' and _nana_ 'without' the third case-affix is employed, optionally (as well as the fifth and the second).

[489] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.33)
SANSKRIT: स्तोक अल्प कृच्छ्र कतिपय इत्येतेभ्योऽसत्ववचनेभ्यः करणे कारकेऽन्यतरस्यां तृतीया भवति पञ्चे पञ्चम्येव ॥
ENGLISH: When expressing an instrument-_karaka_, optionally after the words _stoka_ 'little,' _alpa_ 'little,' _krichchhra_ 'difficulty,' and _katipaya_ 'some,' the fifth case-affix is used, when they do not denote material objects.

[490] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.34)
SANSKRIT: दुरान्तिकार्थैः शब्दैर्योगे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति अन्यतरस्यां पञ्चमी च ॥
ENGLISH: When in conjunction with words having the sense of _dura_ 'distant,' and _antika_ 'near,' the sixth case-affix is optionally employed.

[491] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.35)
SANSKRIT: दूरान्तिकार्थेभ्यः शब्देभ्यो द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति चकारात्पञ्चमीतृतीयापि समुच्चीयते ॥
ENGLISH: After the words having the sense of _dura_ 'distant,' and _antika_ 'near,' the second case-affix is used as well as the fifth and the third.

[492] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.36)
SANSKRIT: सप्तमी विभक्तिर्भवत्यधिकरणे कारके चकाराद्दूरान्तिकार्थेभ्यश्च ॥
ENGLISH: The seventh case-affix is employed when the sense is that of location (I. 4. 45) as well as after the words meaning 'distant' or 'near'.

[493] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.37)
SANSKRIT: भावः क्रिया यस्य च भावेन यस्य च क्रियाक्रियान्तरं लक्ष्यते ततो भावयतः सप्तमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: By the action (_bhava_) of whatsoever, the time of another action is indicated, that takes the seventh case-affix.

[494] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.38)
SANSKRIT: अनादराधिके भावलक्षणे भावयतः षष्ठीसप्तम्यौ विभक्ती भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed (as well as the seventh), when disregard is to be shown, after that by whose action the time of another action is indicated.

[495] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.39)
SANSKRIT: स्वामिन् ईश्वर अधिपति दायाद साक्षिन् प्रतिभू प्रसूत इत्येतैर्योगे षष्ठी सप्तमी विभक्तीभवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth and the seventh case-affixes are used after words when they are joined with _svamin_ 'master,' _isvara_ 'lord,' _adhipati_ 'ruler,' _dayada_ 'an heir', _sakshin_ 'witness,' _pratibhu_ 'a surety,' and _prasuta_ 'begotten'.

[496] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.40)
SANSKRIT: आयुक्तो व्यापारितः कुशलो निपुणः ताभ्यां योगे आसेवायां गम्यमानायां षष्ठी सप्तम्यौ विभक्ती भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: In conjunction with the words _ayukta_ 'engaged,' and _kusala_ 'skilful,' when meaning entire absorption in an engagement, the sixth and the seventh case-affixes are used after a word.

[497] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.41)
SANSKRIT: यतो निर्द्धारण ततः षष्ठीसप्तम्यौ विभक्ती भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth and the seventh case-affixes are used after those words from which specification is made, (as of an individual from the whole class).

[498] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.42)
SANSKRIT: यस्मिन्निर्द्धारणाश्रये विभक्तमस्ति ततः पञ्चमी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥ विभागो विभक्तं ॥
ENGLISH: The fifth case-affix is employed when the thing specified is different or divided from (and not included in) that from which specification is intended.

[499] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.43)
SANSKRIT: साधु निपुण इत्येताभ्यां योगेऽर्चायां गम्यमानायां सप्तमी विभक्तिर्भवति न चेत्, प्रतिः प्रयुज्यते ॥
ENGLISH: In conjunction with the words _sadhu_ 'good,' and _nipuna_ 'skillful,' when they denote respect, the seventh case-affix is employed; provided that the word _prati_ is not used.

[500] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.44)
SANSKRIT: प्रसित उत्सुक इत्येताभ्यां योगे तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवति चकारात्सप्तमी च ॥
ENGLISH: In conjunction with the words _prasita_ 'longing for,' and _utsuka_ 'greatly desirous of,' the third case-affix is used after a word, as well as the seventh.

[501] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.45)
SANSKRIT: लुबन्तान्नक्षत्रशब्दात् तृतीयासप्तम्यौ विभक्तौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: When an affix declaring the time of an Asterism is elided by _lup_ (IV. 2. 4); the seventh and the third case-affixes are used after the word, whose affix is so elided.

[502] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.46)
SANSKRIT: प्रातिपदिकार्थमात्रे लिङ्गमात्रे परिमाणमात्रे वचनमात्रे प्रथमा विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Where the sense is that of the Nominal stem (I. 2. 45) or of gender only, or measure only or number only, the first case-affix is employed.

[503] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.47)
SANSKRIT: संबोधने च प्रथमा विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when the sense is that of addressing, the first case-affix is employed.

[504] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.48)
SANSKRIT: संबोधने या प्रथमा तदन्तं शब्दरूपमामन्त्रितसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The word ending with the first case-affix, in the sense of addressing, is called _Amantrita_ or vocative (VIII. 2. 78).

[505] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.49)
SANSKRIT: आमन्त्रितप्रथमाया यदेकवचनं तत्संबुद्धिसंज्ञं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the sense of vocative, the singular number of the first case-affix is called _Sambuddhi_.

[506] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.50)
SANSKRIT: कर्मादिभ्योऽन्यः प्रातिपदिकार्थव्यतिरिक्तः स्वस्वामिसंबन्धादिः शेषस्तत्र षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed in the remaining case, that is to say, where there is a sense, such as the relation between property and its owner, &c. different from that of a Nominal-stem.

[507] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.51)
SANSKRIT: जानातेरविदर्थस्याज्ञानार्थस्य करणे कारके षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the verb ज्ञा _jna_, when not used in the sense of 'to know,' the instrument takes the sixth case-affix.

[508] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.52)
SANSKRIT: अधीगर्थाः स्मरणार्थाः । दय दानगतिरक्षणेषु । ईश ऐश्वर्ये । एतेषां कर्मणि कारके शेषत्वेन विवक्षिते षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the verbs having the sense of 'remembering,' (_adhik_) and of _daya_ 'to give,' 'to pity' 'to protect,' 'to move,' and of _isa_ 'to rule or be master of,' the object takes the sixth case-affix.

[509] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.53)
SANSKRIT: करोतेः कर्मणि कारके शेषत्वेन विवक्षिते प्रतियत्ने गम्यमाने षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verb _kri_ takes the sixth case-affix, when it means 'to impart a new quality or virtue (1. 3. 32).

[510] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.54)
SANSKRIT: रुजार्थानां धातूनां भाववचनानां भावकर्तृकाणां ज्वरिवर्जितानां कर्मणि कारके शेषत्वेन विवक्षिते षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of verbs having the sense of _ruj_ 'to afflict', with the exception of the Causative verb _jvaraya_ 'to be feverish,' takes the sixth case-affix, when the verb expresses a condition (i. e., when the subject is an Abstract noun).

[511] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.55)
SANSKRIT: नाथृ नाधृ याञ्चोपतापैश्वर्याशीःषु पठ्यते, तस्याशीः क्रियस्य कर्मणि कारके शेषत्वेन विवक्षिते षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the verb _nath_ when meaning 'to bless'; the object takes the sixth case-affix.

[512] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.56)
SANSKRIT: जासि निग्रहण नाट क्राथ पिष् इत्येतेषां धातूनां हिंसा क्रियाणां कर्मणि कारके षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verbs _jasi_ 'to strike'; 'to hurt,' _han_ 'to strike' preceded by नि and प्र, nat_ 'to injure,' '_krath_,' and '_pish_,' when they mean, 'to injure,' takes the sixth case-affix.

[513] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.57)
SANSKRIT: व्यवहृ पण इत्येतयोः समानार्थयोः कर्मणि कारके षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verbs _vyavahri_ and _pan_ when they are synonymous, that is when they mean 'dealing in sale and purchase transactions' or 'staking in gambling,' takes the sixth case-affix.

[514] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.58)
SANSKRIT: व्यवहृपणिसमानार्थस्य दीव्यतेः कर्मणि षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verb _div_ when having the above-mentioned sense of 'dealing' or 'staking,' takes the sixth case-affix.

[515] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.59)
SANSKRIT: उपसर्गे सति दिवस्तदर्थस्य कर्मणि कारके षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verb _div_ when having the above-mentioned sense of dealing or staking, optionally takes the sixth case-affix, when it is preceded by an _upasarga_ (or preposition).

[516] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.60)
SANSKRIT: ब्राह्मणविषये प्रयोगे दिवस्तदर्थस्य कर्मणि कारके द्वितीया विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verb _div_ when having the above-mentioned sense of 'dealing' or 'staking' takes the second case-affix in the _Brahmana_ literature.

[517] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.61)
SANSKRIT: प्रेष्यब्रुवोहविषः कर्मणः षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति देवता संप्रदानेसति ॥
ENGLISH: The object of the verb _preshya_ and _bruhi_ (imperative singular of _Divadi_ verb, meaning 'send' and 'utter,') denoting sacrificial food, takes the sixth case-affix, when making offerings to deity is meant or when deity is the recepient.

[518] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.62)
SANSKRIT: छन्दसि विषये चतुर्थ्यर्थे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति बहुलम् ॥
ENGLISH: In the _chhandas_ the sixth case-affix is employed diversely with the force of the fourth case-affix.

[519] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.63)
SANSKRIT: यजेर्धातोः करणे कारके छन्दसि बहुलं षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is diversely employed in the _chhandas_ in denoting the instrument of the verb _yaj_ 'to sacrifice.'

[520] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.64)
SANSKRIT: कृत्त्वोऽर्थानां प्रत्ययानां प्रयोगे कालेऽधिकरणे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed in denoting location (_adhikarana_) after a word denoting time (_kala_) when used along with a word ending with an affix having the sense of _kritvasuch_ (V. 4. 17) 'so many times.'

[521] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.65)
SANSKRIT: कृत्त्प्रयोगे कर्त्तरि कर्मणि च षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is employed after a word, in denoting the agent and the object, when used along with a word ending with a _krit_ affix (III. 1. 93).

[522] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.66)
SANSKRIT: उभयोः प्राप्तिर्यस्मिन्कृति सोयमुभयप्राप्तिस्तत्रकर्मण्येव षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति, न कतरि ॥
ENGLISH: When the agent and the object of the action denoted by the words formed by _krit_-affixes, are both used in a sentence, in the object only, the sixth case-affix is employed, and not in the agent (the object is put in the genitive case and not the agent).

[523] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.67)
SANSKRIT: क्तस्य वर्तमानकालविहितस्य प्रयोगे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The past participle ending in क्त when used in the sense of the present tense, (III. 2. 187) and (III. 2. 188) is used with the genitive.

[524] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.68)
SANSKRIT: क्तोधिकरणे चेति वक्ष्यति तस्य प्रयोगे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The past participle in क्त is used with the genitive when the former expresses location (III. 4. 76).

[525] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.69)
SANSKRIT: ल उ उक अव्यय निष्ठा खलर्थ तृन् इत्येतेषां प्रयोगे षष्ठीविभक्तिर्न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is not used to express the agent or the object, when the word is governed by an Active Participle ending in the affix ल, or उ, or उक or by an Indeclinable, or by a Past Participle in क्त and क्तवतु, or by a word ending in an affix having the sense of खल् or by a Noun of agency formed by तृन् ॥

[526] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.70)
SANSKRIT: अकस्य भविष्यति काले विहितस्येनस्तु भविष्यति चाधमर्ण्ये च विहितस्य प्रयोगे षष्ठी विभक्तिर्न भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is not used when the word is governed by a verbal noun in अक denoting futurity, or in इन् denoting 'futurity and indebtedness.'

[527] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.71)
SANSKRIT: कृत्यानां प्रयोगे कर्त्तरि वा षष्ठी विभक्तिर्भवति, न कर्मणि ॥
ENGLISH: The sixth case-affix is optionally employed in denoting the agent, (but not the object), when the word is governed by a Future Passive Participle (_kritya_).

[528] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.72)
SANSKRIT: तुल्यार्थैः शब्दैर्योगे तृतीया विभक्तिर्भवत्यन्यतरस्यां, पक्षे षष्ठी च, तुलोपमा शब्दौ वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: The third or the sixth case-affix may optionally be employed, when the word is joined with another word meaning 'like to or resemblance'; excepting तुलां and उपमा

[529] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.3.73)
SANSKRIT: आशिषि गम्यमानायाम् आयुष्य मद्र भद्र कुशल सुख अर्थ हित इत्येतैर्योगे चतुर्थी विभक्तिर्भवति । चकारो विकल्पानुकर्षणार्थः ॥
ENGLISH: The fourth as well as the sixth case-affix may be employed, when blessing is intended in connection with the words _ayusha_ 'long life.' _madra_ 'joy,' _bhadra_ 'good fortune,' _kusala_ 'welfare,' _sukha_ 'happiness,' _artha_ 'prosperity,' and _hita_ 'good.'

[530] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.1)
SANSKRIT: द्विगुः समासः एकवचनं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The compound _Dvigu_ (II. 1. 52) is singular in number.

[531] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.2)
SANSKRIT: प्राण्यङ्गानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति, तथातूर्याङ्गानां सेनाङ्गानां च ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound too is singular in number, when it is compounded of words signifying members of the animal body, players (or singers or dancers) and component parts of an army.

[532] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.3)
SANSKRIT: चरणानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति अनुवादे गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words signifying persons belonging to the different Vedic-Schools When the sense is that of repetition, is singular.

[533] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.4)
SANSKRIT: अध्वर्युक्रतुवाचिनां शब्दानामनपुंसकलिङ्गानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words denoting sacrifices (_kratu_) ordained by the _Yajur_ (_Adhvaryu_) _Veda_, is singular, provided they are never used in the neuter gender.

[534] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.5)
SANSKRIT: अध्ययनेन निमित्तेन येषामविप्रकृष्टा प्रत्यासन्ना आख्या तेषां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words denoting (persons who have studied) subjects, which in their order of study are not remote from each other, is singular.

[535] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.6)
SANSKRIT: जातिवाचिनां शब्दानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति प्राणिनो वर्जयित्वा ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words signifying _jati_ (genus) which are not names of living beings, is singular.

[536] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.7)
SANSKRIT: विशिष्टलिङ्गानां भिन्नलिङ्गानां नदीवाचिनां शब्दानां देशवाचिनां च ग्रामवर्जितानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words of different genders, denoting names of rivers and countries, but not of towns, is singular.

[537] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.8)
SANSKRIT: क्षुद्रजन्तु वाचिनां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words signifying small animals, is singular.

[538] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.9)
SANSKRIT: येषां शाश्वतिको विरोधस्तद्वाचिनां शब्दानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words signifying those animals only among whom there is permanent enmity i.e, natural and eternal antipathy or quarrel, is singular.

[539] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.10)
SANSKRIT: अनिरवसितशूद्रवाचिनां शब्दानां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words denoting those classes of _Sudras_ who have not been expelled from the communion of higher classes, is singular.

[540] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.11)
SANSKRIT: गवाश्वप्रभृतीनि कृतैकवद्भावनि द्वन्द्वरूपाणि साधूनि भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The word _gavasva_, and others are also _Dvandva_ compounds which take the singular number.

[541] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.12)
SANSKRIT: वृक्ष मृग तृण धान्य व्यञ्जन पशु शकुनि अश्ववडव पूर्वापर अधरोत्तर इत्येतेषां द्वन्द्वो विभाषैकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words being the names of trees, wild animals, grass, corn, condiment, domestic beasts and birds, and the compounds _asva_-_vadava_, _purva_-_para_ and _adharottara_, are optionally singular.

[542] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.13)
SANSKRIT: विप्रतिषिद्धार्थानां शब्दानामनधिकरणवाचिनामद्रव्यवाचिनां द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति विभाषानुकर्षणार्थश्चकारः ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound of words of contrary significations, but not being the names of concrete substances, is optionally singular.

[543] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.14)
SANSKRIT: दधिपय आदीनि शब्दरूपाणि नैकवद्भवन्ति ॥
ENGLISH: The _Dvandva_ compounds like '_dadhipaya_' &c., are not singular.

[544] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.15)
SANSKRIT: अधिकरणं वर्त्तिपदार्थः, स हि समासस्यार्थस्याधारः तस्यैतावत्त्वे परिमाणे गम्यमाने द्वन्द्वो नैकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound denoting a fixed number (_etavatva_) of concrete things (_adhikarna_) is not singular.

[545] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.16)
SANSKRIT: अधिकरणैतावत्त्वस्य समीपे विभाषा द्वन्द्व एकवद्भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Dvandva_ compound, denoting an approximate number of concrete substances, is optionally singular.

[546] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.17)
SANSKRIT: यस्यायमेकवद्भावो विहितः स नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति द्विगुर्द्वन्द्वश्च ॥
ENGLISH: It, namely the _Dvigu_ or the _Dvandva_ compound, with regard to which singular number has been ordained by the above rules, is always a neuter gender.

[547] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.18)
SANSKRIT: अव्ययीभावश्च समासो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: An _Avyayibhava_ (II. 1. 6.) compound is also neuter gender.

[548] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.19)
SANSKRIT: नञ्समासं कर्मधारयं च वर्जयित्वाऽन्यस्तरपुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवतीत्येतदधिकृतं वेदितव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound, with the exception of that which is formed by the particle _nan_, and of the _Karmadharaya_ compound, becomes neuter gender, in the cases explained in the following _sutras_.

[549] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.20)
SANSKRIT: संज्ञायां विषये कन्थान्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति , सा चेत् कन्था उशीनरेषु भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When denoting an appellative, a _Tatpurusha_ compound ending with the word _kantha_ 'town,' is neuter in gender, provided that it is the name of a town situated in the country of the _Usinaras_.

[550] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.21)
SANSKRIT: उपज्ञोपक्रमं तदन्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति तदद्याचिख्यासायां तयोरुपज्ञोपक्रमंयोराघिख्यासायां गम्यमानायाम् ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound ending with the words '_upajna_' (invention) and '_upakrama_' (commencement) is neuter in gender, when it is intended to express the starting point of a work which is first invented or commenced.

[551] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.22)
SANSKRIT: छायान्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति बाहुल्ये गम्यमाने ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound ending with the word _chhaya_ 'shadow' is neuter in gender when the sense is that of profuseness of the thing indicated by the first term.

[552] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.23)
SANSKRIT: सभान्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति, सा चेत् सभा राजपूर्वा मनुष्यपूर्वा च भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tatpurusha_ compound ending with the word _sabha_ 'court' is neuter, provided that it is preceded by (a word synonymous with) the word _Raja_, or by a word denoting a non-human being.

[553] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.24)
SANSKRIT: अशाला च या सभा तदन्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound ending with the word _sabha_, when it does not mean a house, but means concourse, is neuter.

[554] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.25)
SANSKRIT: सेना सुरा छाया शाला निशा इत्येवमन्तस्तत्पुरुषो नपुंसकलिङ्गे भवति विभाषा ॥
ENGLISH: A _Tat_-_purusha_ compound ending with the words _sena_ 'army' _sura_ 'wine,' _chhaya_ 'shadow', _sala_ 'house,' and _nisa_ 'night,' is optionally neuter, with the exception of that which is formed by the particle _nan_ (II. 2. 6) and the _karmadharaya_ compound.

[555] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.26)
SANSKRIT: परस्य यल्लिङ्गं तद्भवति द्वन्द्वस्य तत्पुरुषस्य च ॥
ENGLISH: The gender of _Dvandva_ or _Tat_-_purusha_ compound is like that of the last word in it.

[556] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.27)
SANSKRIT: अश्ववडवयोः पूर्ववल्लिङ्गं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The gender of the compound of the word _asva_ 'horse' and _vadava_ 'a mare,' is like that of the first word in it.

[557] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.28)
SANSKRIT: हेमन्तशिशिरौ अहोरात्रे इत्येतयोश्च्छन्दसि विषये पूर्ववल्लिङ्गं भवति ॥
ENGLISH: Of the compounds '_hemantasisirau_' and '_ahoratre_' the gender is like that of the first word; in the _Chhandas_, (vedas).

[558] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.29)
SANSKRIT: रात्र अह्न अह इत्येते पुंसि भाष्यन्ते ॥
ENGLISH: The _Dvandva_ and _Tatpurusha_ compound ending with _ratra_ and _ahna_ and _aha_ are spoken of in the masculine.

[559] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.30)
SANSKRIT: अपथ शब्देः नपुंसकलिङ्गो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: The _Tatpurusha_ word _apatham_ 'a bad road' is neuter.

[560] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.31)
SANSKRIT: अर्द्धार्चादयः शब्दा पुंसि नपुंसके च भाष्यन्ते ॥
ENGLISH: The words '_ardharcha_' &c., are spoken of in masculine and neuter.

[561] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.32)
SANSKRIT: इदमोन्वादेश विषयस्यदेशो भवत्यनुदत्तस्तृतीयादौ विभक्तौ परतः ॥
ENGLISH: In the room of _idam_ 'this,' in case of its re-employment in a subsequent member of the same sentence and referring to the same thing (_anvadesa_) there is the substitution of _as_ which is _anudatta_ i. e., gravely accented, when the third case-affix and the rest follow.

[562] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.33)
SANSKRIT: एतदोऽन्वदेशविषयस्य अशादेशो भवति अनुदात्तस्त्रतसोः परतः ॥ तौ चापि त्रतसावनुदात्तौ भवतः ॥
ENGLISH: In the room of एतद् in case of its re-employment there is the substitution of the gravely accented अश् when the affixes त्र (V. 3. 10) and तस् (V. 3.7) follow, and the affixes त्र and त्रस् are _anudatta_ also (gravely accented.)

[563] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.34)
SANSKRIT: द्वितीया टा ओस् इत्येतेषु परत इदमेतदोरन्वादेराविषययोरेनशब्दआदेशो भवति अनुदात्तः ॥
ENGLISH: When an affix of the 2nd case or टा (Ins. Sing.) or ओश् (Loc, dual) follows, एन which is _anudatta_ is the substitute of इदम् and एतद् in the case of its re-employment.

[564] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.35)
SANSKRIT: यदित ऊर्द्ध्वमनुक्रमिष्यामस्तदार्द्धधातुकेवेदितव्यम् ॥
ENGLISH: The clause 'when the affix is _Ardhadhatuka_' is understood in the following aphorisms up to aphorism 58 of this chapter.

[565] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.36)
SANSKRIT: अदो जग्धिरादेशो भवति ल्यपि परतः तकारादौ च किति प्रत्यये ॥
ENGLISH: The word जग्धि is the substitute of अप् 'to eat,' when the affix ल्यप् (VII. 1. 37) or an _ardhadhatuka_ कित् affix beginning with त follows.

[566] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.37)
SANSKRIT: घस्लृ भावेऽच्युपसंख्यानम् ॥
ENGLISH: When _lun_ (aorist) or _san_ (desiderative) follows घस्लृ is the substitute of अद् 'to eat.'

[567] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.38)
SANSKRIT: घञि अपि च परतोऽदो घस्लृ आदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when the affix घञ् and अप् follow घस्लृ is the substitute of अद्.

[568] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.39)
SANSKRIT: छन्दसि विषये बहुलमदो घस्लृ आदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: In the _chhandas_ (Vedas), घस्लृ is diversely substituted for अद्.

[569] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.40)
SANSKRIT: लिटि परतोऽदोऽन्यतरस्यां घस्ल्रादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When लिट् (Perfect) follows घस्लृ is optionally the substitute of अद्.

[570] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.41)
SANSKRIT: वेञो वयिरादेशो भवति अन्यतरस्यां लिटि परतः ॥
ENGLISH: When लिट् follows, वयि is optionally the substitute of 'वेञ् to weave.'

[571] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.42)
SANSKRIT: हन्तेर्धातोर्वधइत्ययमादेशो भवति लिङि परत आर्द्धधातुके ॥
ENGLISH: वध् is the substitute of हन् 'to kill' when the _ardhadhatuka_ लिङ् (Benedictive) follows.

[572] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.43)
SANSKRIT: लुङि च परतो हनो वध इत्ययमादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when लुङ् (Aorist) follows, वध् is the substitute of हन्.

[573] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.44)
SANSKRIT: पूर्वेण नित्ये प्रति विकल्प उच्यते । आत्मनेपदेषु परतो हनो लुङ्यन्यतरस्यां वध इत्ययमादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When _Atmanepada_ affixes follow, वध् is optionally the substitute of हन् in the लुङ् (Aorist).

[574] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.45)
SANSKRIT: इणो गा इत्ययमादेशो भवति लुङि परतः ॥
ENGLISH: गा is the substitute of the root इण् when लुङ् follows.

[575] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.46)
SANSKRIT: णौ परत इणोऽबोधनार्थस्य गमिरादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: When the affix णि (Causal) follows, गमि is the substitute of इण् when the sense is not that of 'informing' but that of 'going.'

[576] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.47)
SANSKRIT: सनि परत इणोऽबोधनार्थस्य गमिरादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And when सन् (Desiderative) follows, गमि is the substitute of ण् when the sense is not that of 'informing.'

[577] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.48)
SANSKRIT: इङश्च सनि परतो गमिरादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: And of इङ् 'to study,' गमि is the substitute when सन् follows.

[578] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.49)
SANSKRIT: गाङादेशो भवतीङो लिटि परतः ॥
ENGLISH: गाङ् is the substitute of इङ् when लिट् (Perfect) follows.

[579] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.50)
SANSKRIT: लुङि लृङि च परत इङो विभाषा गाङदेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: गाङ् is optionally the substitute of इङ् when लुङ् (Aorist) and लृङ् (Conditional Future) follow.

[580] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.51)
SANSKRIT: णौ सन्परे चङ् परे च परत इङो विभाषा गाङादेशो भवति ॥
ENGLISH: गाङ् is optionally the substitute of इङ् in the causative णि, when that causative takes the affix _san_ (desiderative) and _chan_ (Aorist.)

[581] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.52)
SANSKRIT: अस्तेर्धातोर्भू इत्ययमादेशो भवति आर्द्धधातुके ॥
ENGLISH: भू is the substitute of the verb अस् 'to be,' when an _ardhadhatuka_ affix is to be applied.

[582] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.53)
SANSKRIT: ब्रवो वचिरादेशो भवति आर्द्धधातुके विषये ॥
ENGLISH: वचि is the substitute of ब्रू 'to speak' when any _ardhadhatuka_ affix is to be applied.

[583] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.54)
SANSKRIT: चक्षिङः ख्याञदेशो भवति आर्द्धधातुके ॥
ENGLISH: ख्याङ् is the substitute of चक्षिङ् 'to perceive, to tell,' when any _ardhadhutuka_ affix is to be applied.

[584] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.55)
SANSKRIT: लिटि परतश्चक्षिङः ख्याञादेशो वा भवति ॥
ENGLISH: ख्याञ् is optionally the substitute of चक्षिङ् when लिट् (Perfect) follows.

[585] panini_sutras.jsonl (2.4.56)
SANSKRIT: अजेर्धातोर्वीत्ययमादेशो भवत्यार्द्धधातुके परतो घञपौ वर्जयित्वा ॥
